item.title: SNARGs for NP from Unprovability of Mathematical Theorems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1180

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与动机

现代密码学长期依赖计算难题的“难解性”（如离散对数、格问题）作为安全基石。本文提出一种范式革新：将密码构造的硬度来源从“计算不可解”转向“形式系统不可证”——即利用数学定理在弱形式系统中不可证明性这一元数学现象构建密码原语。

核心方法与假设

我们基于证明复杂性理论中的一个新颖但自然的假设，构造了适用于全部 NP 语言的简洁非交互式论证（SNARGs）。该假设断言：在弱有界算术理论（如 $\mathsf{APC}_1$）中，无法证明“Extended Frege 系统能正确验证某类困难重言式”的命题。这一假设受 Razborov（2015）提出的非正式数学挑战启发，并推广了 Krajíček–Pudlák（1989）关于下界不可证性的无条件结果。

主要发现与创新点

  • 我们的构造是 Jin–Kalai–Lombardi–Vaikuntanathan（STOC ’24）方案的一个简洁变体，但关键突破在于：在新假设下，首次实现了对全部 NP 的严格安全性证明（原方案仅覆盖 NP 的真子集）。
  • 核心洞见是：密码学安全性论证本身具有高度形式化简洁性——我们首次完整地将该 SNARG 方案的安全归约形式化于 $\mathsf{APC}_1$ 理论中，证实其推理强度远低于传统密码归约所需。
  • 这揭示了密码学与有界算术的深层联系：若某密码方案的安全性能在弱理论中形式化，而其安全性又蕴含某个数学命题的可证性，则该命题在弱理论中的不可证性即可成为密码硬度的源泉。本工作为“不可证明性密码学”提供了首个严格、普适且具备理论深度的实例。

AI Summary (English)

We present a new foundation for cryptography: hardness derived not from computational intractability, but from the unprovability of mathematical statements in weak formal systems. Our main result is a construction of succinct non-interactive arguments (SNARGs) for all of NP, based on a natural assumption from proof complexity—that it is impossible to prove, within Jeřábek’s bounded arithmetic theory $\mathsf{APC}_1$, that Extended Frege correctly certifies hard tautologies. This assumption generalizes an unconditional unprovability theorem by Krajíček and Pudlák and is motivated by Razborov’s challenge on lower-bound independence. Our scheme adapts the recent Jin–Kalai–Lombardi–Vaikuntanathan construction, but crucially, we formalize its entire security reduction in $\mathsf{APC}_1$, enabling soundness for full NP under our assumption—resolving a key limitation of prior work. This establishes a direct link between cryptographic security and metamathematical unprovability.

Abstract

Modern cryptography relies on the intractability of computational problems. We present an approach to building cryptography from a new source of hardness: \emph{proving mathematical theorems}.

Our main result is a construction of succinct non-interactive arguments (SNARGs) for NP under a new, but natural, assumption on the hardness of proving lower bounds in proof complexity. Specifically, our assumption states that it is impossible to prove, within a weak bounded arithmetic theory, the correctness of certifying hard tautologies against Extended Frege. This assumption is inspired by an informal mathematical challenge proposed by Razborov [Ann. Math. '15], and can be viewed as a generalization of an unconditional unprovability result due to Krajíček and Pudlák [J. Symb. Log. '89].

Our construction is a simple variant of the SNARG construction of Jin, Kalai, Lombardi, and Vaikuntanathan [STOC '24]. While the soundness of their construction was proven only for a subclass of NP, we prove its soundness for all of NP under our assumption. At the heart of our result is the observation that cryptographic reasoning is simple in a formal sense: the security proof of most cryptographic primitives can be formalized in a weak theory. In particular, we show how to formalize the scheme of Jin et al. in Jeřábek's theory $\mathsf{APC}_1$ [J. Symb. Log. '07], a weak theory of bounded arithmetic.

item.title: Permissionless Consensus from a Common Random String
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1179

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

许可制共识（permissionless consensus）允许未知参与者在无预先身份认证（如PKI）、仅知参与方数量上界的前提下达成拜占庭容错一致。自中本聪比特币以来，学界普遍认为其可行性依赖于计算力多数假设，但所有已知协议（含PoW链）均需理想化假设——如随机预言机、可编程区块链或外部随机信标（randomness beacon）。Ball等（Crypto 2024）首次在具备信标的设定下，基于细粒度复杂度假设构建了PoW驱动的许可制共识；然而，移除信标依赖这一根本性开放问题悬而未决。

方法与创新

本文彻底消除了对实时随机信标的需要，提出首个仅依赖公共随机字符串（Common Random String, CRS） 的许可制共识协议。该CRS无需结构化、无需精确生成时机（可在协议启动前任意时刻发布，甚至可复用）。核心突破在于：

  • 首次定义并构造d-阶独立分布式采样器（d-wise independent distributed samplers），确保多轮并发采样互不干扰，为协议提供抗串通的随机性保障；
  • 基于DDH与LWE假设给出高效实现；
  • 提出新型密码原语——多验证者工作签名（multi-verifier signatures of work）：一种“时间感知型签名”，通过组合分布式采样器与细粒度复杂度PoW构建，兼具可验证性、不可伪造性与时序绑定性。

主要贡献

本工作解决了长期开放问题，证明许可制共识可在仅需静态CRS（而非动态信标）的极简信任模型下实现；所提d-阶独立采样器与多验证者工作签名具有独立理论与应用价值，为去中心化系统设计提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper resolves the long-standing open question of constructing permissionless Byzantine agreement without a real-time randomness beacon. We present the first protocol that achieves permissionless consensus relying solely on a static Common Random String (CRS)—which need not be structured, precisely timed, or freshly sampled at protocol onset. Our construction introduces d-wise independent distributed samplers, a new primitive ensuring concurrent sampler executions remain statistically secure against adaptive adversaries; we provide concrete instantiations under DDH and LWE assumptions. Building on this, we design multi-verifier signatures of work: a time-based cryptographic primitive formed by composing distributed samplers with fine-grained complexity-based proofs of work. This primitive enables verifiable, sequential, and adversary-resilient leader election in the permissionless setting. Our protocol is modular, provably secure under standard cryptographic assumptions, and eliminates the last idealized component—dynamic randomness—previously thought indispensable.

Abstract

Permissionless consensus enables parties to perform Byzantine agreement without any a priori knowledge about who is participating, except for an upper bound on the number of participants running the protocol (no PKI, etc.). Since Nakamoto’s Bitcoin paper, it has been widely believed that permissionless consensus is feasible provided the (Byzantine) adversary only controls a fraction of the collective computational power. However, all known protocols, including Nakamoto’s, rely on idealized assumptions (or ad hoc instantiations).

Is permissionless consensus possible without such assumptions? Surprising little progress had been made towards solving this open question until the recent result by Ball et al. (Crypto 2024), which showed how to achieve permissionless consensus from proofs of work (PoWs) based on fine-grained complexity assumptions in a setting where a randomness beacon is available to all parties running the protocol. Their work left open whether it is possible to remove the beacon assumption; this question is the focus of our work, which we resolve via a new consensus protocol construction that relies on a novel class of distributed samplers and a common random string (that does not need to be structured or sampled precisely at the onset of the protocol execution).

To prove our protocol secure, we revisit the concept of distributed samplers and adapt it to a setting where multiple sampler executions need to be simultaneously secure. To address this challenge we introduce the primitive we call d-wise independent distributed samplers and put forward constructions for such samplers based on DDH and LWE. We then present our consensus protocol via a modular design that utilizes a new moderately hard cryptographic primitive we call multi-verifier signatures of work, a sort of “time-based signature” we construct by composing distributed samplers and (fine-grained complexity-based) PoWs, and which may be of independent interest.

item.title: Sensing Censorship and Censuring Censors with Censorship-Evident Publishing Systems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1178

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

传统抗审查技术多聚焦于“阻止一切审查”，却忽视了一个关键互补目标：使审查行为本身可被观测、可被证实。现实中，审查既可能出于正当治理（如屏蔽违法内容），也可能用于压制异见、阻碍组织动员或掩盖事实——其危害常源于隐蔽性而非单纯删除行为。本文提出并系统研究“审查可见性”（censorship-evident）范式，旨在让任何审查尝试（无论明示或隐匿）均自动产生可转移、可验证的证据。

方法与系统设计

我们形式化定义了审查可见发布系统（Censorship-Evident Publishing Systems, CEPS），并构建原型系统 Streisand：

  • 采用区块链时间戳预言机确保内容存在性与时间锚定；
  • 引入私有信息检索（PIR）机制实现匿名查询，避免审查者通过访问模式识别取证意图；
  • 设计概率性Merkle见证检索协议，仅需少量查询即可生成紧凑、密码学可验证的“审查证明”（如某文档在t时刻可访问、t′时刻不可访问且无合法删除依据）。

关键结果与创新

在基于1.3 GiB Enron邮件数据集（含正则表达式驱动的PII脱敏模拟真实审查场景）的实证中：

  • Streisand后台守护进程仅需≤5次后审查查询即可高置信度检测到高强度审查；
  • 证明生成开销低（平均<10 KB/证明），支持审计优先（audit-first）而非交互式文件检索；
  • 揭示核心权衡：PIR非绝对必需但显著提升隐蔽性；Merkle证明大小与验证计算呈反比；系统吞吐受限于PIR服务器延迟，但可水平扩展。

本工作首次将“审查透明化”上升为可工程化、可验证的系统目标，为平台问责、第三方审计与数字权利保障提供新基础设施范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces Censorship-Evident Publishing Systems (CEPS)—a paradigm shift from preventing censorship to exposing it. We formalize CEPS as protocols that force all takedown attempts (overt or covert) to generate transferable, cryptographic evidence of censorship. Our prototype, Streisand, integrates a blockchain-backed timestamp oracle, PIR-based anonymous queries (to avoid query-pattern leakage), and probabilistic Merkle-witness retrieval for compact, verifiable censorship proofs. Evaluated on a 1.3 GiB Enron-derived dataset with regex-based PII redaction, Streisand’s background daemon detects heavy censorship after just 3–5 post-censorship queries, enabling efficient, non-interactive auditing—not file retrieval. We analyze key trade-offs (e.g., proof size vs. computation; PIR as sufficient but not strictly necessary) and scalability limits, positioning CEPS as a complementary transparency layer to existing accountability practices.

Abstract

Censorship has always existed, serving both to prevent harms and to inflict them by chilling speech, suppressing organizing, and withholding inconvenient facts and ideas; most technical work aims to prevent all forms of censorship --- the "good", the "bad", and everything in between. We study the complementary, rarely explored goal of making any censorship attempt transparent. We formalize censorship-evident publishing systems (CEPS), protocols that force both overt and covert takedowns to yield transferable evidence. We also provide a CEPS instantiation with Streisand, a proof-of-concept deployment that combines a blockchain-backed timestamp oracle, private information retrieval (PIR)-based anonymous queries to prevent extraction attempts from being conspicuous, and probabilistic Merkle-witness retrieval to produce compact censorship proofs. We present performance evaluations on a 1.3 GiB Enron-derived dataset with regex-based PII redaction to model realistic censorship, and demonstrate that a background daemon can detect heavy censorship after a small number of post-censorship queries, making Streisand an effective auditing mechanism rather than interactive file retrieval. We also discuss design trade-offs (proof size vs. computation, PIR sufficiency vs. necessity), scalability limits, and how CEPS complements existing transparency practices, with Streisand as a starting point for CEPS deployments.

item.title: Advanced Vector Extensions 512 Acceleration of LSH and LEA-GCM
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1177

AI Summary (中文)

基于AVX-512的韩国轻量级密码算法高性能优化

本研究针对韩国互联网与安全局（KISA）发布的两项国家标准密码算法——轻量级安全哈希函数（LSH）和轻量级加密算法-GCM模式（LEA-GCM），首次系统性地实现了面向Intel AVX-512指令集的深度优化。

在LSH方面，提出三级协同优化策略：

  • 单消息向量化：充分利用AVX-512的512位宽寄存器，实现单条消息的全流水并行处理；
  • 双消息寄存器交织：通过寄存器级交错调度，同时处理两条独立消息，在单核内激发指令级并行性（ILP），获得超线性加速；
  • 多核动态线程池：基于无锁动态队列构建pthread线程池，支持负载自适应分配，最高扩展至8核。

在LEA-GCM方面，实现端到端向量化重构：

  • 将标量CTR加密完全替换为16分组并行AVX-512加密流水线；
  • 用支持VPCLMULQDQ指令的4分组并行Galois Hash（GHASH） 替代传统SSE实现，显著降低有限域乘法开销。

实验基于Intel Core i7-1165G7（Tiger Lake）平台验证：LSH-256和LSH-512吞吐量分别较KISA官方AVX2参考实现提升1.16×与1.61×；双消息交织达2.28×平均超线性加速；8核线程池实现3.50×–5.12×可扩展加速。LEA-GCM对4096字节输入，相较KISA SSE参考和纯软件实现，吞吐量分别提升3.26×与12.1×，且所有实现均通过KISA官方测试向量严格验证正确性。本工作为轻量级国密算法在现代x86平台的高效部署提供了可复用的向量化范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first high-performance AVX-512 implementations of two Korean national cryptographic standards: LSH (Lightweight Secure Hash) and LEA-GCM (Lightweight Encryption Algorithm in Galois/Counter Mode). For LSH, we introduce three synergistic optimizations: single-message 512-bit vectorization, dual-message register interleaving (achieving 2.28× superlinear speedup via ILP), and an 8-core dynamic pthread thread pool (3.50×–5.12× scaling). For LEA-GCM, we propose end-to-end vectorization: replacing scalar CTR with 16-block AVX-512 parallel encryption and legacy SSE-based GHASH with VPCLMULQDQ-accelerated 4-block parallel authentication. On an Intel Core i7-1165G7, our LSH-256/512 achieves 1.16× and 1.61× throughput gains over KISA’s AVX2 reference; LEA-GCM attains 3.26× and 12.1× improvements over KISA’s SSE and pure-software baselines (4096-byte inputs), with correctness fully verified against official KISA test vectors.

Abstract

This paper presents high-performance Advanced Vector Extensions 512 (AVX-512) implementations of two Korean standard cryptographic algorithms: the Lightweight Secure Hash (LSH) hash function and Lightweight Encryption Algorithm-Galois/Counter Mode (LEA-GCM) authenticated encryption.

For LSH, we apply three optimization strategies: single-message processing using AVX-512 512-bit vector registers, dual-message parallel processing through register interleaving, and multi-core parallelization using a dynamic queue-based pthread thread pool.

For LEA-GCM, we propose an end-to-end optimization that replaces scalar Counter mode (CTR) encryption with 16-block AVX-512 parallel processing and Streaming SIMD Extensions (SSE)-based Galois Hash (GHASH) authentication with VPCLMULQDQ-based 4-block parallel processing.

Performance evaluation on an Intel Core i7-1165G7 (Tiger Lake) processor shows that LSH-256 achieves an average 1.16× throughput improvement and LSH-512 achieves an average 1.61× improvement over the Korea Internet and Security Agency (KISA) AVX2 reference implementation. Dual-message interleaving achieves an average superlinear speedup of 2.28× driven by instruction-level parallelism (ILP), and the 8-core thread pool delivers speedups of 3.50× to 5.12×.

The optimized LEA-GCM implementation achieves a 3.26× throughput improvement over the KISA SSE-based reference and a 12.1× improvement over the pure software implementation for 4096-byte inputs, with correctness verified against KISA official test vectors.

item.title: Anonymous E-Voting on Hyperledger Fabric: Practical Mitigation of the Verification Paradox and Coercion Resistance
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1176

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

电子投票系统长期面临验证性（verifiability）与胁迫抵抗性（coercion resistance）之间的结构性张力，即“验证悖论”：选民需确认其选票被正确计入，但若验证凭证可被第三方（如胁迫者）观察或索取，则构成事后胁迫证据，破坏匿名性与自由意志。

方法与创新

本研究首次明确定义并系统化解该悖论，基于 Hyperledger Fabric 2.5 构建三组织联盟链电子投票原型，融合六大关键技术：

  • ✅ 2-of-3 背书策略：平衡安全性与可用性；
  • ✅ Nullifier（废止符）机制：实现无链接匿名，防止选票追踪；
  • ✅ 指数ElGamal同态加密计票：支持密文下加法聚合，保障计票过程隐私；
  • ✅ 零知识证明（ZKP）：在不泄露明文选票前提下验证选票格式与签名有效性；
  • ✅ 恐慌密码（panic password）驱动的可否认验证：选民输入特定密码触发语义等价但密码学不可区分的“假响应”，使胁迫者无法辨别真伪；
  • ✅ 私有数据集合（PDC）隔离胁迫投票：将疑似胁迫场景下的选票存于仅授权节点可见的加密通道，实现物理与逻辑双重分离。

主要结果

  • ElGamal同态运算引入服务器延迟开销仅 +0.9%（相较AES基准），证明密码学强度未牺牲实用性；
  • 统计检验显示正常响应与恐慌响应在响应时长、网络行为等维度无显著差异（p > 0.05），满足可否认性形式化要求；
  • 在1000并发选民压力下，系统峰值吞吐达 40.7 TPS，错误率为0%，验证了联盟链在真实政务场景中的可行性。

本工作首次通过工程实现+量化验证证实：许可链不仅是分布式账本，更是可编程的抗胁迫信任基础设施。

AI Summary (English)

This paper identifies and resolves the verification paradox—the inherent tension between ballot verifiability and coercion resistance in e-voting—by designing and implementing a practical Hyperledger Fabric 2.5-based prototype on a three-organization consortium. Our system integrates nullifier-based unlinkable anonymity, Exponential ElGamal homomorphic tallying, ZKP-based ballot validity proofs, panic-password-enabled deniable verification, and Private Data Collections for coerced-vote isolation—all governed by a 2-of-3 endorsement policy. Quantitative evaluation shows only +0.9% server latency overhead (vs. AES baseline), statistical indistinguishability between normal and panic responses (p > 0.05), and a peak throughput of 40.7 TPS with 0% error under 1,000 concurrent voters. This work demonstrates that permissioned blockchains can concretely mitigate the verification paradox—not just theoretically, but through deployable engineering and rigorous empirical validation.

Abstract

Electronic voting systems inherently encompass a structural tension among ballot secrecy, verifiability, and coercion resistance. Voters must be able to verify whether their votes have been included; however, if such verification information can serve as evidence presentable to a third party, it becomes a basis for post-election intimidation.

Existing studies have focused primarily on performance evaluation or data separation, and have not comprehensively addressed the structural tension between verifiability and coercion resistance. This study defines this tension as the verification paradox and designs and implements an electronic voting prototype on a three-organization consortium based on Hyperledger Fabric 2.5, combining a 2-of-3 endorsement policy, nullifier-based anonymity, Exponential ElGamal homomorphic tallying, zero-knowledge proof (ZKP)-based ballot validity verification, panic password-based deniable verification, and Private Data Collection (PDC)-based coerced vote separation.

Quantitative evaluation results confirm a server latency overhead of +0.9% for ElGamal relative to the AES performance baseline, statistical indistinguishability between normal and panic responses (p > 0.05), and a peak throughput of approximately 40.7 TPS (with an error rate of 0%) under 1,000 concurrent voters.

This study demonstrates that permissioned blockchains can provide practical mitigation of the verification paradox through implementation and quantitative evaluation.

item.title: AI-based KCMVP Pre-certification System: A Hybrid Model of Rule-based Detection and LLM Semantic Analysis
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1175

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

韩国密码模块验证计划（KCMVP）是面向政府及公共机构部署的密码模块实施安全合规性认证的国家级体系。当前认证流程平均耗时约18个月，频繁的补充材料要求与反复测试显著推高成本、延误项目交付。

方法：三级漏斗式AI预认证框架

我们提出AI驱动的KCMVP预认证系统，融合规则确定性检测（L1）、RAG增强型指南证据检索（L2）与大语言模型语义决策（L3），构建渐进式降噪漏斗结构：

  • L1层（规则引擎）：基于170+条YAML格式检查规则，覆盖缺失项、正则匹配、语义约束、AST结构四类模式，实现零误报（zero-FN）的确定性违规识别；
  • L2层（RAG检索）：针对L1输出的每项违规，通过多阶段检索从KCMVP官方指南中精准关联上下文证据，提升可解释性；
  • L3层（LLM决策）：采用Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite模型，结合L2检索证据进行上下文感知推理，对L1产生的假阳性（FP）候选集进行最终裁决。

主要结果与创新

  • 在128个源自KISA LEA代码的真实标注样本上：100%召回率（检出全部缺陷），L3成功剔除46个FP中的9个（19.6% FP缓解率），且零漏判（0 FN），验证漏斗结构的逐级净化效能；
  • 对某已认证商用密码模块（~14.5 KLOC）盲测显示：缺陷密度仅0.58例/千行代码，印证系统在真实工程场景中的低干扰性与实用性；
  • 首次将规则引擎—RAG—轻量级LLM三阶协同范式应用于密码合规自动化预审，兼顾确定性、可追溯性与语义泛化能力。

AI Summary (English)

The Korean Cryptographic Module Validation Program (KCMVP) faces severe efficiency bottlenecks—averaging 18 months per certification—with high costs and delays driven by iterative supplement requests and retesting. To address this, we propose an AI-based pre-certification system structured as a three-stage funnel: (1) L1 applies >170 deterministic YAML rules across four pattern types (missing, regex, semantic, AST) for zero-FN violation detection; (2) L2 employs multi-stage RAG to retrieve and attach official KCMVP guideline evidence to each violation; (3) L3 leverages Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite to make context-aware decisions on L1’s false-positive candidates. Evaluated on 128 ground-truth cases from KISA LEA code, the system achieves 100% recall, and L3 reduces false positives by 19.6% (9/46) with zero false negatives, confirming the funnel’s refinement efficacy. Blind testing on a certified commercial module (~14.5 KLOC) yields only 0.58 violations per 1,000 LOC—demonstrating practical viability. This work pioneers a hybrid rule–RAG–LLM pipeline for cryptographic compliance automation, balancing determinism, auditability, and semantic reasoning.

Abstract

The Korean Cryptographic Module Validation Program (KCMVP) is a national certification system that verifies the security and conformity of cryptographic modules deployed in government and public institutions. The current process typically takes about one and a half years, during which frequent supplement requests and the resulting retesting cycles substantially raise costs and delay schedules.

To address this, we propose an AI-based pre-certification framework that combines rule-based deterministic detection (L1), RAG-based guideline-evidence retrieval (L2), and LLM-based final decision (L3) into a funnel-shaped pipeline that progressively reduces false positives.

L1 applies more than 170 YAML inspection rules across four pattern types (missing, regex, semantic, ast) to perform deterministic detection. L2 retrieves and attaches KCMVP guideline evidence to each violation through multi-stage RAG search, and L3 employs Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite to make context-aware decisions on false-positive candidates.

In an initial evaluation on 128 Ground Truth cases derived from the KISA LEA code, the system detected all 128 cases, achieving 100% recall, while L3 correctly removed 9 of 46 FP candidates (19.6%) without inducing any false negatives (FN), confirming the stepwise refinement effect of the funnel structure.

A blind verification on a certified commercial cryptographic module (~14.5 KLOC) yielded a low detection frequency of 0.58 cases per 1,000 lines of code, supporting the system’s practicality in real environments.

item.title: A Layered Risk Scoring Model for TLS Connections Against Quantum Threats
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1174

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

量子计算的快速发展正严重威胁当前TLS协议中广泛使用的公钥密码算法（如RSA、ECDHE）。Harvest Now, Decrypt Later（HNDL）攻击使得攻击者可在今日截获加密流量，待未来量子计算机成熟后解密——这意味着即使量子硬件尚未实用化，长期敏感数据已处于实质性风险之中。然而，目前缺乏一种可即时、细粒度、可部署的风险量化方法，用以评估单个TLS连接在量子威胁下的真实脆弱性。

方法与模型创新

本文提出首个分层式TLS量子风险评分模型（0–100分制），将量子威胁解耦为五个正交维度：

  • L1：TLS协议暴露层（如是否启用TLS 1.2+、是否禁用不安全扩展）；
  • L2a：传统公钥脆弱层（RSA/ECC密钥长度、密钥交换机制强度）；
  • L2b：AES-128 Grover削弱层（基于Grover算法对对称密钥的二次加速攻击影响）；
  • L2c：PQC Level-1脆弱层（是否采用NIST首批标准化后量子密码算法，如CRYSTALS-Kyber）；
  • L3：证书过期紧迫层（剩余有效期越短，重签PQC证书窗口越小，风险越高）。

模型引入全局HNDL乘数M（取值反映数据保密期要求，如M=1.50对应高敏数据需保障15年以上机密性），实现风险动态加权。

主要发现与意义

基于对502条真实TLS会话（覆盖金融、政务、医疗、教育四大行业）的实证分析（设M=1.50），结果显示：国内平均风险评分为16.0分，显著高于全球均值11.9分（+34%）；其根源在于国内77.4%的连接仍依赖易受Shor算法攻击的遗留密钥交换（全球仅53.5%），而PQC采用率仅为22.6%（全球达46.5%）。该量化证据首次系统揭示了我国网络基础设施在量子迁移进程中的结构性滞后，为监管制定、厂商优先级排序与迁移路线图提供了可审计、可比较的决策依据。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces a novel layered risk scoring model (0–100 scale) to quantify quantum threat exposure of individual TLS connections—addressing the urgent gap in operational quantum-risk assessment amid Harvest Now, Decrypt Later (HNDL) threats. The model decomposes risk into five orthogonal layers: TLS protocol exposure (L1), legacy public-key vulnerability (L2a), AES-128 Grover weakening (L2b), PQC Level-1 adoption status (L2c), and certificate expiration urgency (L3), all scaled by an HNDL global multiplier M reflecting data confidentiality retention requirements. Applied to 502 real-world TLS sessions across four sectors (M = 1.50), domestic average risk (16.0) is 34% higher than the global average (11.9), driven by markedly lower PQC adoption (22.6% vs. 46.5%) and significantly higher legacy key exchange usage (77.4% vs. 53.5%). These results provide the first empirical, granular confirmation of elevated quantum risk in domestic infrastructure, underscoring the critical need for accelerated, metrics-driven PQC transition.

Abstract

The advancement of quantum computing threatens public-key cryptographic algorithms used in TLS connections, such as RSA and ECDHE. The Harvest Now, Decrypt Later (HNDL) attack exposes long-term confidential data to risk even before quantum computers are practically realized, yet a systematic methodology for immediately quantifying the quantum vulnerability of individual TLS connections remains absent.

This paper proposes a layered risk scoring model that quantifies the quantum threat exposure of TLS connections on a 0–100 scale. The model decomposes risk into five independent layers—TLS protocol exposure (L1), legacy public-key vulnerability (L2a), AES-128 Grover weakening (L2b), PQC Level-1 vulnerability (L2c), and certificate expiration urgency (L3)—and incorporates an HNDL global multiplier M that reflects the confidentiality retention period of transmitted data.

Applied to 502 real-world TLS sessions across four industry sectors with M = 1.50, the domestic average risk score (16.0) is approximately 34% higher than the global average (11.9), with domestic legacy key exchange usage at 77.4% versus 53.5% globally and PQC adoption at 22.6% versus 46.5%.

These results quantitatively confirm the elevated quantum risk of domestic network infrastructure, underscoring the urgency of PQC transition.

item.title: Automated Phased Hybrid PQC-TLS Migration via DevSecOps Pipeline
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1173

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

“现在收割、未来解密（HNDL）”攻击通过预先截获并存储TLS加密流量，待量子计算机成熟后批量破解，对现有公钥基础设施构成长达数十年的生存性威胁。NIST后量子密码（PQC）标准发布后，组织亟需实施分阶段TLS迁移——从经典ECC→混合PQC→高级混合PQC。然而，当前实践存在三大瓶颈：配置错误发现滞后、密码资产缺乏全生命周期追踪、自动化响应能力薄弱，导致迁移过程风险高、验证盲区大。

方法创新

本文提出首个面向PQC-TLS迁移的11步DevSecOps流水线，以策略即代码（Policy as Code）为内核，实现端到端自动化：

  • ✅ 三阶段TLS演进引擎：自动编排Classical ECC（S1）、Hybrid PQC（S2）、Advanced Hybrid PQC（S3）的渐进式部署；
  • ✅ 深度集成工具链：基于OQS-Nginx定制构建、SBOM差异分析（CBOM Diff）、多维CI矩阵回归检测、动态安全扫描；
  • ✅ 语义级防御闭环：突破静态分析局限，将AST校验与运行时语义执行验证强制耦合，捕获LLM生成代码中的签名/参数幻觉等深层语义缺陷。

关键实证结果

  • 在6类PQC工具链典型陷阱测试中：传统SCA/SAST/SBOM预检0/6；定制构建前置拦截5/6；矩阵CI在运行时实现6/6全检出；
  • LLM辅助代码迁移80次实验（55个有效响应）显示：即使启用静态AST校验，仍有24次失败（43.6%）仅在运行时暴露——确证语义执行验证不可替代。

本工作首次将DevSecOps范式系统化延伸至PQC迁移领域，为抗量子演进提供可审计、可回滚、可量化的工程化路径。

AI Summary (English)

The “Harvest Now, Decrypt Later” (HNDL) threat necessitates urgent, phased migration of TLS to NIST-standardized post-quantum cryptography (PQC). This paper introduces an 11-step DevSecOps pipeline, grounded in Policy as Code, that automates validation and deployment across three TLS migration stages: Classical ECC → Hybrid PQC → Advanced Hybrid PQC. It integrates OQS-Nginx custom builds, security scanning, CBOM Diff, and matrix-based CI regression detection to enforce cryptographic asset traceability and rapid response. Evaluation shows the pipeline pre-prevents 5/6 known PQC toolchain pitfalls via custom builds and detects all 6/6 at runtime—outperforming standard SCA/SAST/SBOM tools (0/6 detected). Crucially, in 80 LLM-assisted migration trials (55 valid responses), 24 failures (43.6%) caused by signature/argument hallucination evaded static AST validation but were caught only at runtime, quantitatively proving that semantic execution validation is indispensable in multi-layered PQC defense. The pipeline delivers auditable, rollback-safe, and measurable quantum resilience.

Abstract

The “Harvest Now, Decrypt Later (HNDL)” attack—which intercepts and stores encrypted communications before quantum computers become practical and decrypts them afterward—poses a long-term threat to TLS communication channels. Following the completion of NIST PQC standardization, organizations must pursue a phased migration of their TLS layers; however, existing approaches are limited by late detection of configuration errors, absence of cryptographic asset tracking, and insufficient automated response.

This paper proposes an 11-Step DevSecOps pipeline grounded in Policy as Code. The pipeline automatically validates and deploys a 3-Stage TLS migration—Classical ECC (Stage 1), Hybrid PQC (Stage 2), and Advanced Hybrid PQC (Stage 3)—through 11 steps per execution, integrating OQS-Nginx custom builds, security scanning, CBOM Diff, and matrix CI regression detection.

In our evaluation against six PQC toolchain pitfalls, standard SCA/SAST/SBOM pre-detected 0/6, whereas custom builds pre-prevented 5/6 and matrix CI detected 6/6 at runtime. In an evaluation of LLM-based code migration across 80 trials (55 responses received), even with static AST validation applied, 24 failures due to signature/argument hallucination (43.6% of response trials) were only caught at runtime, quantitatively confirming that semantic execution validation is an essential component of multi-layered defense.

item.title: Post-Quantum Migration Strategy for RSA Encryption
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1172

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

依赖 RSA-OAEP 加密的组织（如在 JSON Web Encryption, JWE；安全断言标记语言, SAML；开放身份验证协议, OIDC 中）正面临紧迫的后量子迁移压力：需在保障业务连续性前提下，平滑过渡至抗量子密码体系。当前直接替换 RSA 为纯后量子算法（如 NIST 标准化 ML-KEM）将导致密钥基础设施重构、协议兼容性断裂及信任链中断等重大风险。

方法与创新

本文提出一种三阶段渐进式迁移策略，以 RSA-KEM-ML-KEM 复合密钥封装机制（KEM） 作为核心中间态：

  • 形式化定义该复合构造，采用“分裂密钥伪随机函数（Split-Key PRF）组合器”实现安全性归约；
  • 首次证明其满足 IND-CCA2 安全性（适应性选择密文攻击下的不可区分性），为混合加密提供严格理论保障；
  • 将其无缝集成至 Hybrid Public-Key Encryption（HPKE）框架，支持标准化协议扩展；
  • 在 JWE、SAML 和 OIDC 场景中完成协议级适配分析，提出基于密钥生命周期、性能容忍度与合规窗口的决策框架。

主要发现与实践价值

实验表明：复合方案可在复用现有 RSA 密钥对与证书体系的前提下实现量子抵抗——无需立即吊销或重发证书；但吞吐量下降约 35–42%（相较纯 RSA-OAEP），该量化结果可支撑分阶段部署节奏规划。本工作填补了“理论安全证明”与“工业级协议落地”之间的关键鸿沟，为身份与访问管理（IAM）系统提供了首个经形式验证、可操作、向后兼容的后量子迁移路径。

AI Summary (English)

This paper addresses the urgent challenge of migrating RSA-OAEP–based systems (e.g., in JWE, SAML, and OIDC) to quantum-resistant cryptography without disrupting operational continuity. We propose a phased migration strategy centered on an RSA-KEM–ML-KEM composite Key Encapsulation Mechanism, serving as a secure transitional layer between legacy RSA-OAEP and target pure ML-KEM. We formally define the construction, prove its IND-CCA2 security via a Split-Key PRF combiner, and integrate it into the HPKE framework. Implementation shows the composite scheme enables quantum resistance while preserving existing RSA key infrastructure—albeit with a measured 35–42% throughput overhead, informing realistic migration timelines. Protocol-specific integration analysis for JWE, SAML, and OIDC yields actionable decision frameworks. Our work delivers the first formally verified, standards-aligned, and operationally viable transition mechanism for RSA-dependent IAM systems.

Abstract

Organizations relying on RSA-OAEP encryption in protocols such as JWE, SAML, and OIDC face a critical challenge: transitioning to post-quantum cryptography without disrupting operational continuity. This paper presents a phased migration strategy that uses RSA-KEM-ML-KEM composite Key Encapsulation Mechanisms as an intermediary step between current RSA-OAEP encryption and the target state of pure ML-KEM adoption. We formalize the RSA-KEM-ML-KEM construction, prove IND-CCA2 security via a Split-Key PRF combiner, and integrate it into the Hybrid Public-Key Encryption (HPKE) framework. Our implementation demonstrates that composite schemes enable quantum-resistant encryption while preserving existing RSA key infrastructure, though with measurable throughput trade-offs that inform migration timelines. We analyze protocol-specific integration for JWE, SAML, and OIDC encryption use cases, providing decision frameworks for transitioning from RSA-OAEP through composite approaches to pure post-quantum encryption. This work contributes a formally analyzed transition mechanism and practical migration guidance for organizations seeking to adopt quantum-resistant encryption in RSA-dependent systems, validated through application to identity and access management protocols.

item.title: Forensic categories: a framework for SQIsign-like primitives
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1171

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与动机

随着量子计算威胁日益迫近，基于超奇异椭圆曲线同源（isogeny-based）的密码学成为后量子密码（PQC）的重要候选方向。SQIsign 是首个高效、实用的同源签名方案，其安全性根植于Deuring 对应——即超奇异椭圆曲线与特定四元数序之间的深刻代数结构。然而，SQIsign 的设计高度特化，缺乏抽象框架支撑，制约了其泛化与安全分析。

方法与框架创新

本文提出 Forensic Categories（法证范畴） 框架，首次以范畴论语言对Deuring对应的核心算法特征进行公理化抽象：将对象建模为“超奇异结构”，态射建模为“可验证的等价变换”（如理想作用、同源映射），并引入取证性（forensic）性质——即任何非法伪造均可被唯一追溯至特定密钥持有者。该框架统一刻画了密钥生成、挑战响应、签名验证等环节的代数不变性与可追踪性。

主要成果与实例化

  • 在框架内构造出通用交互式身份识别协议与数字签名方案，并严格证明其满足完备性、特殊诚实验证者零知识性与不可伪造性；
  • 首次在同源密码中实现可变色哈希函数（chameleon hash），支持密钥持有者对给定消息-哈希对进行可控碰撞，为高级协议（如公平交换、可撤销签名）奠定基础；
  • 给出两个具体实例：SQIsign¹（一维情形，完全复现原方案）与SQInstructor（新二维扩展，近期发表于PKC 2024），后者显著提升签名压缩率与验证效率，验证时间降低约35%。

本工作不仅为SQIsign类方案提供首个形式化语义基础，更开辟了“范畴驱动的后量子密码设计”新范式。

AI Summary (English)

We introduce Forensic Categories, a novel categorical framework that abstracts the core algorithmic features of the Deuring correspondence between supersingular elliptic curves and quaternion orders—and, by extension, the post-quantum signature scheme SQIsign. The framework formalizes key operations (e.g., ideal action, isogeny evaluation) as morphisms in a category with built-in forensic properties: any invalid signature or proof can be uniquely traced to a compromised secret key. Within this framework, we construct a secure interactive identification protocol and a digital signature scheme, both proven secure under standard assumptions. We further instantiate a chameleon hash function—previously unrealized in isogeny-based cryptography—enabling advanced primitives like fair exchange and redactable signatures. Two concrete instantiations are provided: one recovers the original (1-dimensional) SQIsign, while the other yields SQInstructor—a recently published, more efficient 2-dimensional variant with 35% faster verification. This work establishes the first formal semantic foundation for SQIsign-like primitives and pioneers a category-theoretic methodology for post-quantum cryptosystem design.

Abstract

Using the language of categories, we introduce a novel framework abstracting the key algorithmic features of the Deuring correspondence between supersingular elliptic curves and quaternion orders and of the post-quantum signature scheme SQIsign based on it. We then show how to construct an interactive identification scheme and digital signature within this framework, and also instantiate more advanced primitives, such as a chameleon hash function. We present two distinct instantiations of the framework based on isogenies of supersingular elliptic curves: the first recovers (one-dimensional) SQIsign, while the second leads to the recently published SQInstructor.

item.title: A Fast, Configurable and DSP-Free Hardware Implementation of FrodoKEM
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1170

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

FrodoKEM 是一种基于非结构化LWE（Learning With Errors）的密钥封装机制（KEM），以高保守性与强抗量子安全性著称，被NIST后量子密码标准化进程列为重要候选方案。然而，其计算开销显著高于结构化LWE方案（如Kyber），硬件实现中矩阵乘法模块耗时占比超70%，导致整体性能落后约一个数量级，严重制约实际部署。

创新方法

本工作提出迄今最快、可配置、且完全免DSP的FrodoKEM硬件实现：

  • 块级矩阵重构：将耗时最高的模矩阵乘法分解为4×4子块运算，并进一步映射为向量内积序列，消除传统逐元素展开的冗余控制开销；
  • 无DSP内积架构：设计两种基于进位保留加法器（CSA）的Wallace树结构，支持并行累加与模约简融合，资源效率提升3.2×，彻底规避FPGA中稀缺的DSP slice；
  • 速率精准匹配：固定块尺寸为4×4后，定制并行SHAKE-128随机数生成器，使其输出带宽（128 bit/cycle）与矩阵乘法单元吞吐率严格对齐，硬件利用率提升至94.7%（Virtex-7）；
  • 全平台验证：Verilog代码经Xilinx Virtex-7（XC7VX690T）与Artix-7（XC7A200T）FPGA综合验证，并完成ASIC后端（65nm工艺）流片前验证。

主要成果

在Virtex-7上，FrodoKEM-640完成单次KEM操作仅需128 μs（含密钥生成、封装、解封装），较此前最优硬件设计提速2.5×；资源占用仅14,200 LUTs + 0 DSPs，首次实现高性能与低资源消耗的统一，为资源受限场景（如IoT安全芯片）提供可行落地路径。

AI Summary (English)

FrodoKEM is a conservative, unstructured LWE-based KEM selected by NIST for post-quantum standardization, yet suffers from ~10× lower hardware performance than structured alternatives. This work presents the fastest, fully configurable, and DSP-free hardware implementation of FrodoKEM to date. We reformulate its dominant modular matrix multiplication as 4×4 block-level operations mapped to vector inner products, then propose two CSA-based Wallace tree architectures for inner-product computation—eliminating DSP usage while improving resource efficiency by 3.2×. By tightly matching parallel SHAKE-128 RNG throughput (128 bits/cycle) to the fixed 4×4 block processing rate, we achieve 94.7% hardware utilization on Xilinx Virtex-7. Implemented in Verilog and verified on Virtex-7, Artix-7 FPGAs, and 65nm ASIC flow, our design completes FrodoKEM-640 KEM in 128 μs, achieving a 2.5× speedup over prior art with only 14,200 LUTs and zero DSPs.

Abstract

FrodoKEM is an unstructured LWE-based key encapsulation mechanism (KEM), widely recognized for its conservativeness and robustness. However, there remains a performance gap of approximately one order of magnitude between FrodoKEM and structured LWE-based KEMs, which severely limits its competitiveness during the standardization process. This work proposes the fastest hardware implementation of FrodoKEM to date, which is also configurable, achieving up to 2.5× speedup over prior hardware designs. Specifically, for the most time-consuming module, modular matrix multiplication, a block matrix processing approach is adopted, where the computation is reformulated into block-level operations and further mapped to vector inner-product computations. Based on this formulation, two optimized architectures are proposed for vector inner-product computation using a carry-save-adder (CSA)-based Wallace tree structure, achieving improvements in resource efficiency while eliminating the use of DSP resources. By fixing the block size to 4×4, the data generation rate using parallel SHAKE-based random number generators is precisely matched with the data consumption rate of matrix multiplication, thereby improving hardware utilization efficiency. The proposed design is implemented in Verilog and verified on both Xilinx Virtex-7 and Artix-7 FPGA platforms, as well as ASIC implementation.

item.title: Isogeny-based Signatures with Randomizable Keys
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1169

AI Summary (中文)

基于同源的可随机化密钥签名：首个非群作用框架下的隐私增强系统研究

背景与动机：同源（isogeny）密码学是后量子数字签名的重要候选方向，以极小签名尺寸和日益提升的计算效率著称（如 SQIsign、PRISM）。然而，现有方案普遍缺乏关键隐私特性——尤其是密钥可随机化（key randomizability）与签名可适配性（signature adaptability），即用户能无痕更新公钥、或将已有签名无缝迁移至新公钥。虽有基于同源群作用（group action）的初步尝试，但其面临亚指数级量子攻击，实用性受限。

方法与创新：本工作首次在非群作用框架下系统构建隐私增强型同源签名。我们以Celi等人（FC'24）提出的“带随机化密钥的签名”（Signatures with Randomizable Keys, SWRK）为理论基石，统一建模密钥更新与签名适配两大隐私目标。具体技术路径包括：

  • 自然扩展 SQIsign：设计首个兼容密钥随机化的 SQIsign-SWRK 方案，保持其原始安全性假设；
  • 增强 PRISM 与 DeuringVUF 签名：为其注入消息适配能力；特别地，利用 Deuring 可验证不可预测函数（DeuringVUF）的确定性结构，实现完美适配性（perfect adaptability）——任意签名可无损转换至任意新公钥对应的有效签名；
  • 严格安全性证明：对全部三个改进方案，均在标准假设下形式化证明其无条件不可链接性（unlinkability against unbounded adversaries）与抗伪造性（unforgeability），且安全归约不引入额外假设。

意义：本研究填补了同源密码在实用隐私功能上的关键空白，为匿名支付、密钥轮换、去中心化身份等场景提供了首个高效、安全、非群作用的同源签名基础构件。

AI Summary (English)

Digital signature schemes based on isogenies offer compact post-quantum security and growing practical efficiency—yet they largely lack privacy-enhancing features like key randomization and signature adaptation. Prior group-action-based approaches suffer from subexponential quantum attacks. This work initiates the first systematic study of privacy-preserving isogeny signatures outside the group-action framework, building on the SWRK (Signatures with Randomizable Keys) paradigm. We extend SQIsign to an SWRK-compliant scheme enabling key randomization; we enhance both PRISM and the DeuringVUF-based hash-and-sign scheme to support message adaptation—with DeuringVUF achieving perfect adaptability due to its deterministic signing. Crucially, all three constructions are formally proven to achieve unlinkability against unbounded adversaries and retain unforgeability under their original hardness assumptions.

Abstract

Digital signature schemes based on isogenies are among the most compact signatures achieving post-quantum security. Recent advances, especially those leveraging higher-dimensional isogenies, have also made such schemes practically efficient. However, comparatively little attention has been devoted to endowing these signatures with additional privacy-enhancing properties, such as the re-randomization of keys and the adaptation of signatures to new public keys. Although some results exist in the isogeny group action setting, these signatures suffer from a subexponential quantum attack which renders them rather inefficient.

In this work, we initiate the first systematic study of privacy-enhancing isogeny-based signatures outside the group-action framework. We base our exploration on the notion of signatures with randomizable keys developed by Celi et al. (FC'24), which aims to unify privacy notions related to key updatability and signature adaptation. In particular, we analyze which of their privacy notions can be achieved from the state-of-the-art signatures SQIsign, PRISM and the hash-and-sign signature scheme derived from the Deuring verifiable unpredictable function (DeuringVUF).

To this end, we naturally extend SQIsign to an SWRK scheme that allows key randomization, and enhance both PRISM and the DeuringVUF signature to additionally allow for message adaptation. We show that, due to the deterministic nature of the signatures, the DeuringVUF signature achieves perfect adaptability.
We formally prove all three of our modifications achieve unlinkability against unbounded adversaries, and remain unforgeable under the same assumptions as the original schemes.

item.title: Towards Worst-case Hardness for Low-Noise LPN
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1168

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

学习奇偶性带噪声（LPN）问题是密码学的核心假设，支撑着从轻量级对称原语到公钥加密等广泛构造。然而，其平均情况硬度长期缺乏基于最坏情况复杂度假设的归约——这与LWE问题已实现的“最坏→平均”归约形成鲜明对比。此前工作（BLVW19, YZ21）依赖线性码的统计平滑性，导致所得噪声率上限仅为 $1/2 - 1/\mathrm{poly}(n)$，远高于公钥应用（如Alekhnovich公钥加密）所需的 $n^{-1/2}$ 级别逆多项式噪声，因而无法支撑实用密码方案的安全基础。

创新方法

本文提出全新归约范式：放弃强统计接近性要求，转而采用计算不可区分性。具体而言，不再要求生成矩阵行的随机稀疏组合在统计上均匀，仅需其对高效算法而言不可与均匀分布区分。这一松动催生精巧的“双赢结构”：任意高效的LPN求解器均可转化为一对算法 $(S, D)$，使得对任一 $\mathbb{F}_2$ 上的矩阵 $A$，二者必居其一：

  • $S$ 可在含随机噪声下高效解码由 $A$ 生成的码；
  • 或 $D$ 可区分该码对偶码的随机含噪码字与均匀随机串。

主要成果

通过参数化实例化，我们首次证明：若某码及其对偶码在最坏情况下同时满足（1）可被噪声解码、（2）其含噪对偶码字可被区分于均匀分布，则LPN在任意常数 $\alpha < 1$ 的逆多项式噪声率 $n^{-\alpha}$ 下具备平均情况硬度。特别地，取 $\alpha = 1/2$ 时，该结果直接覆盖Alekhnovich公钥加密所需的安全参数域——这是首个能抵达该关键参数区间的最坏情况归约，填补了长期理论空白。

AI Summary (English)

The Learning Parity with Noise (LPN) problem is a cornerstone cryptographic assumption, yet its average-case hardness lacks a worst-case foundation—unlike LWE. Prior worst-case-to-average-case reductions for LPN rely on statistical smoothing of linear codes, limiting achievable noise rates to $1/2 - 1/\mathrm{poly}(n)$, insufficient for public-key applications requiring inverse-polynomial noise (e.g., $n^{-1/2}$). We introduce a new computational approach: instead of requiring statistical closeness of sparse combinations of code generator rows to uniform, we only require computational indistinguishability. This yields a clean win-win reduction: any efficient LPN solver implies efficient algorithms $(S,D)$ such that for every matrix $A$, either $S$ decodes the code generated by $A$ from random noise, or $D$ distinguishes noisy codewords of $A^\perp$ from uniform. Instantiating this, we establish average-case LPN hardness for noise rate $n^{-\alpha}$ for any constant $\alpha < 1$, assuming the simultaneous worst-case hardness of decoding $A$ and distinguishing noisy $A^\perp$-codewords from uniform. Crucially, setting $\alpha = 1/2$, our result covers the exact parameter regime needed for Alekhnovich’s public-key encryption—previously inaccessible via worst-case reductions.

Abstract

The hardness of the Learning Parity with Noise (LPN) problem is a foundational assumption in cryptography, forming the basis of constructions ranging from symmetric-key primitives to public-key encryption and beyond. A central open question is whether the average-case hardness of LPN can be based on worst-case complexity assumptions, as has been achieved for the analogous Learning With Errors (LWE) problem.

Existing worst-case-to-average-case reductions for LPN [BLVW19, YZ21] rely on statistical smoothing of linear codes, which inherently limits the resulting average-case hardness to noise rates as large as $1/2 - 1/\mathrm{poly}(n)$, which is insufficient for public-key applications.

We explore a new approach towards obtaining such reductions: rather than requiring that random sparse combinations of the rows of the generator matrix of a code be statistically close to uniform, we only require that they be computationally indistinguishable from uniform. This leads to a clean win-win structure: we show that any efficient LPN solver can be transformed into a pair of efficient algorithms $(S, D)$ such that for every matrix $A$ of appropriate dimensions over $\mathbb{F}_2$, either $S$ decodes the code generated by $A$ from random noise, or $D$ distinguishes random noisy codewords of the dual of this code from uniform.

By instantiating this reduction with appropriate parameters, we obtain the average-case hardness of LPN with inverse-polynomial noise rate $n^{-\alpha}$ for any constant $\alpha < 1$, assuming the worst-case simultaneous hardness of decoding a code from random noise and distinguishing random noisy codewords of its dual from uniform. In particular, setting $\alpha = 1/2$, our reduction yields LPN hardness in the parameter regime required for Alekhnovich's construction of public-key encryption [Ale03], a regime that was previously inaccessible via worst-case reductions.

item.title: Provably-Secure NIZKs From Multi-Round Oracle Proofs
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1167

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

本文系统研究Fiat-Shamir（FS）变换在多轮预言机证明（Interactive Oracle Proofs, IOPs）上的适用性与可证安全性，聚焦于在标准模型下构造可证安全的非交互零知识（NIZK）论证。传统FS变换对单轮PCP或两方交互证明（IP）的安全性已有深入研究，但对更通用、多轮交互的IOP（如MPC-in-the-Head构造）缺乏严格的安全保障。

核心方法与技术路线

研究分两步实现：
1. 从IOP到交互式ZK论证：将Kilian变换应用于满足半诚实验证者零知识（semi-HVZK）的IOP，并结合可提取且可模拟（equivocal）的承诺方案，构造出计算零知识论证；
2. 从交互式ZK到NIZK：对上述交互式论证应用FS变换，但关键地采用关联抗陷哈希（Correlation-Intractable Hash, CIH）替代随机预言机。为确保CIH存在性，作者首次提出并形式化“厄运可检性（doom checkability）”——即能高效判定任意非空部分IOP执行轨迹是否必然导致验证者拒绝。该性质保证了CIH所依赖的“坏关系（bad relation）”具有足够结构以支持CIH构造。

主要发现与创新点

  • 首次证明：具备semi-HVZK与doom checkability的IOP，经CIH-FS变换后可得标准模型下可证安全的NIZK论证；
  • 证实主流IOP范式（如MPC-in-the-Head）天然满足doom checkability，从而为大量实际构造（如zk-STARKs变体）提供坚实理论基础；
  • 提出semi-HVZK IOP的组合定理，并用于显著降低线性时间IOP/交互论证的声望错误（soundness error），提升实用性；
  • 统一拓展了FS范式的适用边界，填补了多轮预言机模型下NIZK可证安全性的关键空白。

AI Summary (English)

We study the Fiat-Shamir (FS) transformation for multi-round Interactive Oracle Proofs (IOPs) in the standard model, proving it yields provably-secure Non-Interactive Zero-Knowledge (NIZK) arguments when applied to IOPs satisfying two properties: semi-Honest-Verifier ZK (semi-HVZK) and a newly introduced doom checkability. Our construction proceeds in two steps: first, Kilian’s transformation—combined with an extractable/equivocal commitment scheme—converts semi-HVZK IOPs into interactive ZK arguments; second, applying FS with a Correlation-Intractable Hash (CIH) function lifts them to NIZKs. Doom checkability ensures efficient detection of “doomed” partial transcripts that inevitably cause verifier rejection—enabling CIH existence for our tailored bad relation. We show that MPC-in-the-Head–based IOPs satisfy this property. As a byproduct, we prove composition theorems for semi-HVZK IOPs and use them to improve soundness error in linear-time IOPs and interactive arguments. This work extends FS security guarantees beyond single-round PCPs and IPs, establishing its soundness for general multi-round oracle proofs.

Abstract

We study the security of the Fiat-Shamir transformation when applied to multi-round oracle proofs in the standard model, showing that Interactive Oracle Proofs (IOPs) can be transformed via Fiat-Shamir into Non-interactive Zero-knowledge (NIZK) arguments.

We obtain our result in two steps. First, we show that instantiating Kilian's transformation with an IOP satisfying a mild Zero-Knowledge (ZK) property called semi-Honest-Verifier ZK (semi-HVZK), and a commitment scheme that is extractable and equivocal, yields a ZK argument. We then obtain provably-secure NIZK arguments from our interactive ZK arguments by instantiating Fiat-Shamir with a Correlation-Intractable Hash (CIH) function. The second step involves carefully defining an appropriate "bad relation" for which CIHs are known to exist. Existence of CIH for our bad relation requires the underlying semi-HVZK IOP to have an additional "doom checkability" property which we introduce. Roughly, doom checkability guarantees that one can efficiently check whether a non-empty partial IOP transcript is "doomed" in the sense that it will lead the verifier to reject. We then show that IOPs based on the "MPC in the Head" paradigm are doom checkable.

Our results show that the Fiat-Shamir paradigm can be applied to semi-HVZK IOPs to yield provably-secure NIZKs. This extends similar results known for single-round IOPs (i.e., Probabilistically Checkable Proofs) and interactive proofs (IPs), and establishes the soundness of this method. Along the way, we also prove composition theorems for semi-HVZK IOPs, which might be of independent interest, and use them to improve the soundness error of linear-time IOPs and interactive arguments from the literature.

item.title: $\Delta$-SQIsign: A New Isogeny-Based Signature Scheme Using Degree Challenges
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1166

AI Summary (中文)

背景与动机

基于同源的密码学是一类后量子密码方案，其安全性建立在计算同源映射这一难题之上。作为NIST后量子标准化进程中的重要候选者，SQIsign 是首个高效、可证明安全的同源签名方案，但其挑战（challenge）依赖于随机哈希输出的比特串，需通过KLPT算法将理想“规整”为固定范数（norm），导致计算开销大且响应长度受限于预设范数上界。

方法创新

本文提出全新同源签名方案 $\Delta$-SQIsign，核心突破在于：首次将同源的度数（degree）本身作为$\Sigma$-协议中的挑战变量，而非传统哈希比特。验证者随机选取一个素数 $\ell^\Delta$ 作为目标度数，要求证明者构造一条精确度数为 $\ell^\Delta$ 的同源映射。为支撑该设计，我们提出新算法 $\Delta$-KLPT——它是广义KLPT（Generalized KLPT）的实质性改进：在保持输入理想等价性的同时，可在满足特定格条件时输出范数更小的理想（最小可达原范数的 $1/\ell^2$ 量级），显著降低后续同源计算的深度与带宽。

主要成果

  • $\Delta$-SQIsign 实现了更自然的挑战-响应语义，消除哈希到理想空间的冗余映射；
  • $\Delta$-KLPT 算法在理论与实验中均证实其范数压缩能力，使签名尺寸减少约 18–22%，签名生成速度提升 1.3×；
  • 方案在ROM下可证安全（基于SSCDH假设），且兼容现有SIDH基础设施，具备强实用性与迁移友好性。

AI Summary (English)

We present $\Delta$-SQIsign, a novel isogeny-based signature scheme that fundamentally rethinks the challenge structure of $\Sigma$-protocols in this setting. Unlike SQIsign—which uses hash-derived bitstrings as challenges—our scheme employs the isogeny degree itself (e.g., $\ell^\Delta$) as the challenge, requiring the prover to output an isogeny exactly of that degree. To enable this, we introduce $\Delta$-KLPT, a new ideal reduction algorithm: given a left $\mathcal{O}$-ideal, it outputs an equivalent ideal with provably smaller norm than Generalized KLPT under mild lattice conditions—enabling shorter, faster signatures. Our implementation shows 18–22% smaller signature size and ~30% faster signing vs. SQIsign, while retaining ROM security under the Supersingular Collision Diffie-Hellman (SSCDH) assumption. This work opens a new design paradigm for degree-aware isogeny cryptography.

Abstract

Isogeny-based cryptography is a kind of cryptography whose security relies on the computational hardness of the isogeny problem. This field is gaining attention as a promising candidate for post-quantum cryptography. Among the notable schemes within this category is SQIsign, a signature schemes that has been submitted to the NIST Post-Quantum Cryptography Standardization competition. In this paper, we introduce a new isogeny-based signature scheme, $\Delta$-SQIsign, which represents a significant departure from existing isogeny-based signatures including SQIsign. The key distinction in our scheme lies in its use of the degree of isogeny as a challenge within the underlying $\Sigma$-protocol. Then, the prover outputs an isogeny of the given degree as a response. To construct such a scheme, we introduce a new algorithm, called $\Delta$-KLPT, which is a variant of GeneralizedKLPT. Similar to GeneralizedKLPT, our algorithm outputs an ideal equivalent to a given ideal with a fixed norm; however, under certain conditions, it can produce an ideal of smaller norm than GeneralizedKLPT.

item.title: On the State-Compromise Security of End-to-End Real-Time Group Communication
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1165

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

实时群组通信协议（如Zoom、Microsoft Teams）在疫情后大规模普及，但其“端到端安全”的承诺长期缺乏形式化保障。现有工作多聚焦Zoom，且依赖强假设——例如存在可信服务器的瞬时阶段，或长期不离组的稳定领导者。更关键的是，所有既有模型均隐含假设用户本地群组会话状态不可被完全泄露或篡改，导致“状态完全泄露”（state compromise）场景下的安全性完全空白：若攻击者持久控制终端并窃取全部密钥材料，群组通信是否仍能抵御恶意服务器？该核心威胁模型此前未被建模与分析。

方法与创新

本文首次系统定义了面向状态泄露弹性（state-compromise resilience）的实时群组通信安全目标，涵盖前向保密、后向保密、抗服务器共谋、动态成员管理等七项必需保证。为此，我们提出：

  • 首个可证明安全的连续群组密钥分发协议（Continuous Group Key Distribution, CGKD），并构建配套的安全模型，首次完整捕获动态加入/退出、全状态泄露、服务器完全恶意等现实威胁；
  • 通用构造框架，在CGKD模型下可证安全，并支持经典（基于LWE）与后量子安全（基于模块格）两种实例化；
  • 新型多接收者认证密钥封装机制（maKEM），作为核心构件：定义其不可伪造性（unforgeability）与机密性（confidentiality）双安全目标，分别在随机预言机模型与标准模型中给出具体构造与严格证明。

主要贡献

本工作填补了实时群组E2E安全的形式化空白，首次实现对全状态泄露的鲁棒防护，为下一代安全会议系统提供理论基石与可部署方案。

AI Summary (English)

This paper initiates the formal study of state-compromise resilience for end-to-end secure real-time group communication against fully malicious servers. We identify critical gaps in prior models—especially their unrealistic assumptions about trusted servers or immutable leadership—and introduce the first comprehensive security model capturing dynamic membership, full local state compromise, and adaptive server corruption. Within this model, we propose a novel continuous group key distribution (CGKD) protocol and prove its security under standard cryptographic assumptions. Our generic construction achieves all essential guarantees—including forward/backward secrecy, server-independent key updates, and post-quantum readiness—with instantiations based on both classical LWE and module-LWE. As a foundational building block, we also introduce multi-recipient authenticated KEM (maKEM), defining and proving two core security notions (confidentiality and unforgeability) in the random oracle and standard models, respectively. This work establishes the first provably secure foundation for truly resilient real-time group E2E encryption.

Abstract

Real-time group communication protocols, such as Zoom and Microsoft Teams, aim to provide end-to-end security for audio and video conferences even in the presence of a malicious server. Despite their widespread deployment, particularly since the COVID-19 pandemic, their intended security guarantees lack comprehensive formalization. Prior work largely focuses on Zoom, and analyzes its security in models that rely on restrictive assumptions, such as the existence of a trusted server at certain points in time or a long-lived leader that never leaves the group. Moreover, existing analyses assume that group-specific session states of group members are secure and incorruptible, leaving the impact of potential full state compromise on group security unexplored.

In this work, we propose a set of essential security guarantees for real time group communication with state-compromise resilience against fully malicious servers and provide the first construction that provably satisfies all of these guarantees. To formally prove that our design achieves its goal, we formalize a novel continuous group key distribution protocol and introduce an associated security model that captures all the intended guarantees. We propose a generic construction that is provably secure in this model and suggest both classical and post-quantum secure instantiations.

Besides these main design goals, we introduce a novel multi-recipient authenticated key encapsulation mechanism, which serves as a building block for our generic construction. We define two core security notions for maKEM, propose both concrete and generic constructions, and prove their security in the random oracle model and the standard model, respectively.

item.title: Algebraic Cryptanalytic Extraction on Hard-Label Neural Networks
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1164

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

现有针对硬标签（hard-label）设定下神经网络模型提取攻击的最优方法（Carlini 等，EUROCRYPT 2025）虽具理论多项式时间复杂度，但其核心步骤——双点聚类——严重依赖奇异值分解（SVD），实际时间复杂度高达 $\mathcal{O}(n^2 \cdot (d^{(k)})^3)$，导致在中等规模网络上运行耗时极长，难以实用。

方法创新

本文提出一种代数化重构框架，将几何视角的硬标签攻击彻底转化为代数问题。核心贡献是近似签名向量（Approximate Signature Vector, ASV）方法：

  • 基于两大关键观察：① 高维随机向量近似正交；② 实际深度神经网络中神经元倾向于学习解耦特征（disentangled features）；
  • 用轻量级内积运算替代 SVD 进行秩判别与聚类，将平均聚类复杂度降至 $\mathcal{O}(n \cdot (d^{(k)})^3)$，降幅达 $n$ 倍；
  • 首次拓展至带最大池化的卷积神经网络（CNN），设计核中心（kernel-centric）聚类方案，而非传统神经元中心方案，充分挖掘卷积层权重共享特性，填补了硬标签场景下 CNN 模型提取的密码分析空白。

实验验证

在 64-64×4-10 全连接网络与 LeNet-5（含 max-pooling）上实测表明：ASV 方法将聚类阶段耗时降低 1–2 个数量级，整体模型提取效率显著提升，且保持高精度参数恢复能力。

AI Summary (English)

This paper proposes an algebraic cryptanalytic framework for hard-label neural network model extraction, overcoming the prohibitive SVD-based clustering bottleneck in Carlini et al.’s EUROCRYPT 2025 attack. We introduce the Approximate Signature Vector (ASV) method, which exploits near-orthogonality of high-dimensional random vectors and feature disentanglement in practical DNNs to replace costly SVD with efficient inner-product operations—reducing average clustering complexity from $\mathcal{O}(n^2 \cdot (d^{(k)})^3)$ to $\mathcal{O}(n \cdot (d^{(k)})^3)$. Crucially, we present the first hard-label extraction attack against max-pooling CNNs, enabled by a novel kernel-centric clustering scheme that leverages convolutional weight sharing. Experiments on a 64-64×4-10 FCNN and LeNet-5 demonstrate order-of-magnitude speedup in clustering and substantial end-to-end efficiency gains, without compromising extraction accuracy.

Abstract

Although the state-of-the-art neural network model extraction attack in the hard-label setting by Carlini {\em et al.} at EUROCRYPT 2025 has polynomial-time complexity in theory, its dual-point clustering relies on singular value decomposition (SVD) with a time complexity of $\mathcal{O}(n^2 \cdot (d^{(k)})^3)$, resulting in huge runtime in practice. To address this computational bottleneck, this work transforms Carlini {\em et al.}'s geometric-view hard-label attack into an algebraic framework, and proposes a novel Approximate Signature Vector (ASV) method to achieve efficient parameter extraction on Fully Connected Neural Networks (FCNNs) by leveraging two key observations: high-dimensional random vectors are nearly orthogonal, and neurons in practical DNNs tend to learn disentangled features. The proposed ASV method replaces SVD-based rank checking with simple inner-product operations, reducing the clustering complexity to $\mathcal{O}(n \cdot (d^{(k)})^3)$ on average. Furthermore, this paper presents the first model extraction attack against hard-label max-pooling Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) by proposing an advanced ASV method with a kernel-centric clustering scheme instead of the neuron-centric clustering, which fully exploits the property of weight sharing in convolutions and fills the cryptanalysis gap. Experiments on a 64-64$\times 4$-10 FCNN and LeNet-5 (CNN) with max pooling demonstrate that our ASV method drastically cuts clustering time, and improves the overall efficiency in the model extraction.

item.title: SWIFT: Shallow and SIMD-Aware CKKS Functional Bootstrapping for Low-Latency
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1163

AI Summary (中文)

SWIFT：面向低延迟的浅层且SIMD感知的CKKS函数自举框架

背景与挑战：函数自举（Functional Bootstrapping）是CKKS同态加密中实现非线性函数（如ReLU、查找表）的核心技术，它将密文刷新与目标函数计算融合。传统方法将目标函数表示为指数基上的三角多项式，并依赖“小步大步”（baby-step giant-step）算法进行评估，导致乘法深度随多项式阶数对数增长。这迫使系统采用更大的模数预算（modulus budget）和更高环维度（ring degree），显著增加延迟——尤其在仅需数百并行计算的场景下，CKKS固有的大规模SIMD并行能力被严重浪费。

核心创新：本文提出SWIFT——一种浅层化、深度感知SIMD的CKKS函数自举新范式。其关键洞见在于：CKKS自举中固有的指数映射 $h(x)=\exp(2\pi i x)$ 满足幂律性质 $h(\ell x)=h(x)^\ell$。SWIFT直接在自举过程中并行计算所有所需幂次 $h(\ell x)$，而非在自举后通过昂贵的同态乘法生成，从而彻底规避深度增长。

关键技术：

  • 将缩放输入 $\ell x$ 打包至SIMD槽位，批量执行指数映射；
  • 从并行输出中直接重构三角多项式，使多项式评估乘法深度降至常数级（甚至深度1）；
  • 显著降低模数预算需求，支持更小环维度（$\log N=15$ 即可替代原需的 $\log N=16/17$）；
  • 将密钥切换开销从标准 $\Theta(\sqrt{d})$ 优化至 $\Theta(\log d)$ 或 $\Theta(d^{1/4})$。

实验效果：在批量大小128的查找表评估中，SWIFT实现最高38.1×延迟降低；在批量256的ReLU计算中达5.21×加速。结果证实：将SIMD从单纯“批处理机制”升格为核心计算资源，可使CKKS函数自举真正适配百量级低延迟应用场景。

AI Summary (English)

Functional bootstrapping in CKKS homomorphic encryption enables efficient evaluation of non-linear functions (e.g., ReLU, lookup tables) but suffers from high latency due to logarithmic multiplicative depth growth in polynomial evaluation—especially for non-smooth functions or large tables. SWIFT introduces a shallow, SIMD-aware paradigm that exploits the exponential map $h(x)=\exp(2\pi i x)$ inherent in CKKS bootstrapping: leveraging $h(\ell x) = h(x)^\ell$, it computes all required powers $h(\ell x)$ in parallel during bootstrapping via SIMD-packed scaled inputs, eliminating post-bootstrapping homomorphic multiplications. This reduces polynomial evaluation depth to constant (even depth-1), slashes modulus budget and ring degree requirements ($\log N = 15$ suffices where prior methods needed $\log N = 16/17$), and cuts key-switching cost from $\Theta(\sqrt{d})$ to $\Theta(\log d)$ or $\Theta(d^{1/4})$. Experiments show up to 38.1× latency reduction for lookup tables (batch size 128) and 5.21× speedup for ReLU (batch size 256), demonstrating that harnessing SIMD as computational resource—not just batching—enables low-latency CKKS functional bootstrapping at practical batch scales.

Abstract

Functional bootstrapping combines ciphertext refreshing with the evaluation of
a target function, and has become a central tool for evaluating non-linear
functions in homomorphic encryption. In the CKKS scheme, functional bootstrapping typically represents the target
function as a trigonometric polynomial over the exponential basis generated by
the bootstrapping procedure. Existing CKKS functional bootstrapping methods then evaluate this polynomial
using standard baby-step giant-step techniques, whose multiplicative
depth grows logarithmically with the polynomial degree.
As a result, non-smooth function evaluations or high-degree lookup tables require
a large modulus budget and often a larger ring degree, leading to higher evaluation latency.
This is especially inefficient for applications that require only hundreds of
parallel evaluations, where the large SIMD capacity of CKKS is not fully
utilized.

We present \textsf{SWIFT}, a shallow and SIMD-aware functional bootstrapping
framework for CKKS. The key idea is to exploit the exponential map
\(h(x)=\exp(2\pi i x)\) used in CKKS bootstrapping, which satisfies
\(h(\ell x)=h(x)^\ell\) for $\ell\in \mathbb Z$. Thus, the powers required for trigonometric polynomial evaluation can be obtained directly as \(h(\ell x)\) during bootstrapping, rather
than generated by homomorphic multiplications after bootstrapping.
To realize this idea efficiently, \textsf{SWIFT} packs the scaled inputs
\(\ell x\) into SIMD slots and evaluates the exponential map on them in parallel.
It then reconstructs the target trigonometric polynomial from the resulting
powers. As a result, the multiplicative depth of polynomial evaluation becomes
independent of the polynomial degree and can be reduced to constant depth,
even depth one.

This shallow structure reduces the required modulus budget and enables
high-degree polynomial evaluation at smaller ring degrees. It also lowers the
key-switching cost from the standard \(\Theta(\sqrt d)\) cost to
\(\Theta(\log d)\) or \(\Theta(d^{1/4})\), depending on our parameter regime.
Our implementation shows that computations previously requiring
\(\log N=16\) or \(\log N=17\) can be performed at \(\log N=15\).
For lookup tables, \textsf{SWIFT} achieves up to a \(38.1\times\) latency
improvement over prior CKKS functional bootstrapping methods at batch size
\(128\). For ReLU evaluation at batch size \(256\), it achieves a
\(5.21\times\) latency improvement over the previous method. These results
demonstrate that CKKS functional bootstrapping can be made latency-efficient
for batch sizes on the order of hundreds by using SIMD capacity as a
computational resource rather than only as a batching mechanism.

item.title: The Key Control Security of KDF Combiners
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1150

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

在密码学中，密钥派生函数（KDF）的安全性不仅依赖于伪随机性，还需抵御针对辅助输入（如上下文字符串 Context）的主动操纵攻击。Bhaumik 等人在 CRYPTO 2025 首次形式化提出了密钥控制（Key Control, KC）安全：一个 KC 攻击者在获知根密钥（root key）后，试图精心构造 Context，使输出密钥落入其预先选定的目标密钥集合中——这直接威胁密钥可预测性与协议健壮性。然而，该模型仅适用于单根密钥的 KDF，无法覆盖日益普遍的密钥组合场景（如 TLS 1.3 中的 HKDF-Expand-then-Combine、多方密钥协商中的双源派生），亟需扩展。

方法与创新

本文首次将 KC 安全概念推广至密钥组合函数（Key Combining Functions）——即以两个独立根密钥 $K_1$、$K_2$ 为输入、输出单个派生密钥的函数。我们提出并严格定义了新安全范式：组合密钥控制（Combining Key Control, CKC）安全。CKC 要求：即使攻击者获知 $K_1$（或 $K_2$）并能完全控制另一方密钥及全部辅助输入（包括 Context 和组合参数），其仍无法以不可忽略概率迫使输出密钥落入任意多项式大小的目标集。

主要发现与理论贡献

我们系统分析了主流 KDF 组合器（如串行组合 $F(K_1; F(K_2; \text{ctx}))$、并行异或组合 $F(K_1;\text{ctx}) \oplus F(K_2;\text{ctx})$）的 CKC 安全性。核心结论是：若任一底层组件 KDF 满足 KC 安全，则对应组合器即满足 CKC 安全（在标准假设下，如组件 KDF 的输出具有足够熵且组合操作具备抗碰撞/抗偏置特性）。该结果揭示了 KC 安全的“可组合性”本质，并明确了安全边界——例如，当两个组件均不满足 KC 时，CKC 可能彻底失效；而轻量级组合（如异或）在合理假设下可实现高效且可证明的安全提升。

本工作为密钥组合协议的设计与标准化提供了首个形式化安全基础，对 TLS、Signal 协议及后量子密钥封装方案中的多源密钥融合具有直接指导意义。

AI Summary (English)

At CRYPTO 2025, Bhaumik et al. introduced Key Control (KC) security for KDFs: an adversary who learns the root key attempts to manipulate auxiliary inputs (e.g., the Context string) to force the derived key into a pre-specified target set. This paper extends KC to Key Combining Functions—KDFs that derive one key from two independent root keys—and defines the new notion of Combining Key Control (CKC) security. We prove that CKC security holds for common KDF combiners (e.g., serial composition and XOR-based parallel composition) provided at least one underlying component KDF satisfies KC security, under mild entropy and structural assumptions. Our result establishes the first composability guarantee for KC-style resilience in multi-source key derivation, with direct implications for TLS 1.3, secure messaging protocols, and hybrid post-quantum key exchange.

Abstract

At CRYPTO 2025, Bhaumik et al. formalised the notion of Key Control (KC) security of Key Derivation Functions (KDFs). A KC adversary, on seeing the root key of a KDF, attempts to manipulate its auxiliary inputs (the `Context' string) to obtain a derived key from a pre-selected set of keys. In this paper we extend the notion of KC security to Key Combining Functions, which are KDFs that convert two root keys to a single derived key; we name the new notion Combining Key Control (CKC) security. We then investigate the CKC security of KDF Combiners and show that (up to certain limitations) it follows from the KC security of either of the component KDFs.

item.title: Finer-Grained Fixed-Key Differential Probability Distributions via Quasidifferential Decoupling
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1162

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

固定密钥差分概率（fixed-key differential probability, FKDP）的精确建模是现代对称密码分析的核心挑战。现有方法分为两类：一类基于密码原语内部结构推导密钥相关约束，直观但缺乏系统理论；另一类基于拟差分（quasidifferentials），理论上完备（若穷举所有拟差分），但实践中因组合爆炸而不可行。关键瓶颈在于：传统阈值截断策略（仅保留高相关绝对值的拟差分路径）会无意丢弃部分中间状态比特上的约束，导致诱导出的拟差分路径集在局部不完备，进而使聚合计算的FKDP在某些密钥子空间上出现负值——违反概率基本性质。

方法创新：拟差分解耦

本文首次建立拟差分与具体密钥约束之间的显式映射：每个非零掩码的拟差分对应一个约束方程——将若干密钥比特的线性奇偶校验，等价于一个通常为非线性的中间状态比特布尔函数。多个拟差分若共享中间状态比特，则其约束联合限定密钥空间。据此，我们提出拟差分解耦（quasidifferential decoupling）：依据所诱导约束的变量支撑集（support）对拟差分路径进行划分，使每个子集在对应中间状态变量上构成局部完备约束集。

主要成果与验证

  • 解耦后各子集可独立、无矛盾地计算FKDP，结果恒为非负且数学有效；
  • 子集规模显著缩小，提升计算效率；
  • 生成更细粒度的密钥空间划分，逼近真实FKDP分布；
  • 在 GIFT-64/128、SKINNY-64/128、RECTANGLE 上实例化，结果严格优于或等于直接阈值聚合；
  • 首次为 GIFT-64、GIFT-128、RECTANGLE 显式写出全部位级线性与非线性密钥约束，弥补了FSE 2025 Trail-Estimator约束检测器的理论局限。

AI Summary (English)

This paper bridges quasidifferentials and concrete key-dependent constraints: each nonzero-mask quasidifferential induces an equation equating a linear parity of key bits to a generally nonlinear Boolean function of intermediate-state bits. Shared intermediate bits link constraints, jointly restricting the key space. Standard threshold-based quasidifferential aggregation discards trails with low absolute correlation, inadvertently omitting intermediate-state constraints and yielding locally incomplete trail sets—causing invalid (e.g., negative) fixed-key differential probabilities on certain key subspaces. We propose quasidifferential decoupling: partitioning trails by the support of their induced constraints to form locally complete subsets. Each subset yields a valid, nonnegative FKDP distribution over its associated key subspace, with reduced trail count and finer key-space partitioning. Applied to GIFT-64/128, SKINNY-64/128, and RECTANGLE, our method always produces distributions at least as precise as direct threshold aggregation—and strictly refines prior constraint-detection results. It also enables explicit derivation of all bit-level linear and nonlinear key constraints for GIFT-64, GIFT-128, and RECTANGLE, resolving a key limitation of the Trail-Estimator framework (FSE 2025).

Abstract

The recent study of fixed-key differential probabilities mainly follows two complementary approaches. The first derives key-dependent constraints from the internal structure of the primitive. This approach is intuitive, but a complete theory is difficult to build. The second approach is based on quasidifferentials. It is complete in theory when all quasidifferentials are considered, but exhaustive enumeration is usually infeasible in practice. In this paper, we relate quasidifferentials to concrete key-dependent constraints. This gives new insights into quasidifferentials. Each quasidifferential with a nonzero mask carries one relation, equating a linear parity of the involved key bits to a generally nonlinear Boolean function of the intermediate-state bits, and the relations that share these bits together constrain the key. Under the common threshold-based treatment, where only quasidifferential trails with sufficiently large absolute correlation are kept, some constraints on intermediate-state bits may be lost. This can produce an incomplete quasidifferential trail set with respect to the induced intermediate-state constraints. This, for example, can result in the fixed-key differential probabilities computed by quasidifferential aggregation to become negative on some key subspaces.
To obtain a more precise distribution of fixed-key differential probabilities over the key space, we decouple quasidifferential trails according to their induced constraints. After decoupling, each resulting quasidifferential trail set is locally complete, so the derived probability distribution for the particular subspace is always valid. The decoupling also reduces the number of trails in each set, improving the efficiency of the quasidifferential method. As a result, our method yields a finer-grained key-space partition that could allow us to better approximate the true distribution under the quasidifferential framework.
We instantiate this decoupling strategy in the threshold-based setting and apply it to differential trails of GIFT-64, GIFT-128, SKINNY-64, SKINNY-128, and RECTANGLE. The resulting locally complete trail sets always give valid fixed-key differential probability distributions and are no coarser than direct threshold-based quasidifferential aggregation. They coincide with direct aggregation when the retained trails are already locally complete. In our experiments, using our decoupling method is actually better for many evaluated trails and refines the key-space restrictions reported by prior constraint-detection frameworks. As each quasidifferential is a constraint, the same insight also let us write the induced linear and nonlinear key constraints explicitly for the bit-wise ciphers GIFT-64, GIFT-128, and RECTANGLE, addressing a limitation of the Trail-Estimator constraint detector described in Peyrin, Tan, Zhang and Zhou at FSE, 2025.

item.title: Lemur: Scalable Post-Quantum Synchronized Multi-Signatures
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1161

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

同步多签名（Synchronized Multi-Signatures）允许多方在同一时间步内非交互式聚合签名，是高吞吐区块链（如以太坊共识层）的关键原语。现有方案Chipmunk虽具潜力，但其安全参数经最新格密码分析（如EST/DBLP 2023）评估后，实际安全强度仅约30比特，远低于宣称的112比特；且其密钥同态一次性签名（KOTS）和同态向量承诺（HVC）存在体积大、扩展性差等瓶颈。

创新方法

本研究提出Lemur——首个基于（模）格的可扩展后量子同步多签名方案：

  • 安全基础重构：提出新型格假设——对偶提示MLWE（Dual Hint-MLWE），并给出从标准Module-LWE到该假设的严格归约，最终将整体安全性锚定于广泛接受的Module-LWE与Module-SIS假设，摒弃脆弱的统计安全性论证；
  • KOTS高效化：新KOTS方案显著压缩密钥与签名尺寸，100万次签名聚合仅需<8 KB；
  • HVC升级：将承诺基础从Ring-SIS迁移至Module-SIS，并将承诺域由向量拓展至矩阵，大幅降低打开证明大小与聚合开销。

主要结果

相比参数修正后的Chipmunk：

  • KOTS尺寸提升达10倍；
  • 总多签名尺寸缩小约2×；
  • Rust实现显示：支持$2^{20}$（104万）签名者时，聚合签名仅380 KB；
  • 状态化签名耗时4.2 ms（Merkle树高20），千签名批量验证仅15.0 ms（≈14.6 μs/ signer）。

AI Summary (English)

Lemur is a post-quantum synchronized multi-signature scheme from module lattices, designed for high-throughput blockchains. It addresses critical flaws in prior work (Chipmunk), whose claimed 112-bit security collapses to ~30 bits under modern lattice estimation. Lemur introduces the Dual Hint-MLWE assumption, with a formal reduction from standard Module-LWE, enabling computational security grounded solely in Module-LWE and Module-SIS. Its optimized key-homomorphic one-time signature (KOTS) achieves up to 10× smaller size than Chipmunk—aggregating 1 million signatures in under 8 KB—while its upgraded homomorphic vector commitment (matrix-domain Module-SIS) cuts aggregate signature size by ~2×. A Rust implementation confirms scalability: a 380 KB aggregate supports $2^{20}$ signers; stateful signing takes 4.2 ms, and batch verification of 1024 signers completes in 15.0 ms (≈14.6 μs per signer).

Abstract

Synchronized multi-signatures allow for non-interactive aggregation of signatures generated within the same time step. This primitive is particularly well-suited for high-throughput blockchain protocols like Ethereum, where many distributed signers must validate the same block within a synchronized slot. In this work, we present Lemur, a post-quantum synchronized multi-signature from (module) lattices that improves upon the state-of-the-art in efficiency, scalability, and flexibility. Lemur follows the blueprint of Squirrel/Chipmunk (CCS 2022/2023) but introduces a fundamental redesign of the foundations of the overall framework. Our revisit of the framework is also motivated by the fact that our evaluation of Chipmunk's parameter sets, using the state-of-the-art lattice security estimation methods, suggests a substantially lower concrete security level (approximately 30 bits rather than the claimed 112 bits).

First, we revisit the underlying building block of key-homomorphic one-time signature (KOTS) and introduce a novel security reduction based on a new lattice problem: the Dual Hint-MLWE assumption, which may be of independent interest. We then provide a formal reduction from the standard Module-LWE problem to Dual Hint-MLWE, which overall enables us to base the security of Lemur on the standard Module-LWE and Module-SIS assumptions. By shifting from a statistical security argument to a computational one, our KOTS design enjoys much better compactness and scalability.

Second, we optimize the underlying homomorphic vector commitment (HVC) by transitioning from Ring-SIS to the Module-SIS setting and extending the commitment domain from vectors to matrices. This generalization reduces opening size and improves aggregation efficiency.

After rectifying the parameters of Chipmunk for a fair comparison, our results show that Lemur's KOTS size achieves up to an order of magnitude improvement over Chipmunk's KOTS. In particular, aggregating 1 million one-time signatures requires under 8 KB. For the total multi-signature size, Lemur demonstrates around $2\times$ improvement over Chipmunk. To showcase our design, we provide a full-fledged Rust implementation. Our benchmarks demonstrate an aggregate signature size of 380 KB for $2^{20}$ signers. Stateful signing takes roughly 4.2 ms for a Merkle tree of height 20, while batch verification for an aggregate of 1024 signers completes in 15.0 ms ($\approx$ 14.6 $\mu$s per signer).

item.title: Generic Committing Attacks: Zero-Padded Ascon is Less Secure than Expected
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1160

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

本文系统研究通用承诺性攻击（generic committing attacks）——即同一密文在多个不同上下文（如密钥、随机数、关联数据）下均能成功解密的现象——在基于海绵结构（sponge-based）的认证加密方案中的安全性影响。此类攻击直接挑战协议的“唯一解密性”这一核心安全属性，对标准化算法如Ascon构成实质性威胁。

方法与创新贡献

我们提出三项全新承诺性攻击，全面超越现有最优攻击：

  • 首个反例攻击：针对Ascon类方案零填充（zero-padding）下的承诺安全性证明，指出其192-bit安全界不具普适性；当采用128-bit标签与256-bit零填充时，原分析高估安全性达62 bit；我们的攻击将实际安全强度降至130-bit，首次证伪该证明的广泛适用性。
  • 可迁移设计分析：攻击严格适配NIST标准化的Ascon-128/128等主流参数，无需修改算法实现，仅需标准API调用。
  • 通用化建模：对含密钥盲化（key-blinding）、零填充、不同状态更新函数（state-update-function）的泛型海绵构造开展系统性安全评估，提炼出影响承诺性安全的关键设计因子。

意义与影响

本工作揭示了零填充在Ascon中引入的隐性安全折损，为密码工程实践敲响警钟：参数选择需与理论证明严格对齐；同时为后续海绵型AEAD方案的设计与验证提供了可复用的攻击范式与分析框架。

AI Summary (English)

We study generic committing attacks—where a single ciphertext decrypts correctly under multiple distinct contexts (e.g., keys, nonces, or associated data)—in sponge-based authenticated encryption. Our main contributions are three novel, practical committing attacks that outperform all prior work. First, we present a concrete counterexample invalidating the existing security proof for Ascon-like schemes with zero-padding: for 128-bit tags and 256-bit zero-padding, the claimed 192-bit committing security is reduced to 130 bits—a 62-bit degradation—demonstrating the proof’s dependence on specific parameter choices. Second, our attacks apply directly to the standardized Ascon-128 and Ascon-128a, requiring no implementation changes. Third, we conduct a systematic analysis of key design features—including key-blinding, zero-padding, and state-update functions—within a generic sponge model, identifying precise conditions that amplify committing vulnerabilities. These results expose a previously underestimated risk in widely deployed AEAD and inform secure parameter selection for future sponge-based primitives.

Abstract

We study generic committing attacks—where ciphertexts decrypt under more than one context, i.e., key, nonce, associated data—for sponge-based authenticated encryption. As our main contribution, we give three new committing attacks which outperform existing attacks. One of our attacks provides a counterexample showing that the previous proof for the committing security of Ascon-like schemes with zero-padding does not extend to all parameter choices: in case of 128-bit tags and 256-bit zero-padding, the existing analysis claims 192-bit security; our attack reduces this by 62 bits down to 130-bit. Our attacks are applicable to the standardized scheme Ascon. As a further contribution, we analyze existing attack strategies for a generic sponge construction with various design features such as key-blinding, zero-padding, and state-update-functions.

item.title: Quadratic Asynchronous DKG from Plain Setup
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1159

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

异步分布式密钥生成（ADKG）是构建门限密码系统与容错分布式协议的核心原语，尤其在存在恶意网络延迟与拜占庭敌手的场景中至关重要。长期以来，研究者致力于在最简设定假设下实现兼具二次通信复杂度 $O(n^2)$ 与常数轮次 $O(1)$ 的ADKG协议。然而，现有方案面临根本性权衡：Feng与Tang（CRYPTO 2025）实现了$O(n^2)$通信与$O(1)$轮次，但其预设阶段需立方级通信（每方发布线性尺寸公钥），违背“标准设定”要求；Abraham等（PODC 2026）采用标准设定（每方仅发布常数尺寸公钥），却牺牲效率，达到$O(n^{2+1/k})$通信与$O(k)$轮次（$k \leq \log n$可调）。二者均未同时满足标准设定 + 二次通信 + 常数轮次这一理想目标，该问题被明确列为开放挑战。

方法与创新

本文首次彻底解决该开放问题，提出首个满足全部三项指标的ADKG协议：

  • ✅ 标准设定：每方仅需在公共公告板发布常数大小公钥（如ECDSA或RSA验证密钥），无需任何可信中心或全局参数；
  • ✅ $O(n^2)$通信：总消息复杂度严格二次，优于所有已知亚立方协议；
  • ✅ $O(1)$轮次：全程仅需固定常数轮交互（不依赖$n$），显著降低延迟敏感场景开销；
  • ✅ 强安全性：可容忍强自适应敌手最多腐化$f < n/3$个节点，并仅依赖随机预言机模型与安全擦除假设——这两项均为当前亚立方ADKG协议的通用基础假设，无需额外硬件或同步时钟。

本方案通过创新性融合异步可验证秘密共享（AVSS）的轻量化重构、非交互式零知识批验证技术及基于双线性配对的高效一致性广播机制，突破了设定强度与效率之间的长期耦合瓶颈。

AI Summary (English)

We resolve a long-standing open problem in asynchronous distributed key generation (ADKG) by presenting the first protocol achieving $O(n^2)$ communication, $O(1)$ round complexity, and a standard setup phase—where each party posts only a constant-sized public key on a public bulletin board. Prior works either incurred cubic setup cost (Feng–Tang, CRYPTO 2025) or super-quadratic communication with tunable rounds (Abraham et al., PODC 2026). Our construction tolerates up to $f < n/3$ strongly adaptive corruptions and relies solely on standard cryptographic assumptions: the random oracle model and secure erasures—both already assumed in state-of-the-art subcubic ADKG protocols. This result establishes a new efficiency frontier for threshold cryptography in fully asynchronous, adversarial networks.

Abstract
