Asynchronous distributed key generation (ADKG) is a fundamental primitive for building threshold cryptosystems and fault-tolerant distributed protocols in adversarial network settings. A central objective in this line of work is to achieve ADKG with $O(n^2)$ communication and constant round complexity under minimal setup assumptions. Recent progress has led to subcubic-communication ADKG protocols under different trade-offs. Feng and Tang (CRYPTO 2025) presented an ADKG protocol with $O(n^2)$ communication and $O(1)$ rounds, but at the cost of a cubic-communication setup phase in which each party posts a linear-sized public key on a public bulletin board. In contrast, Abraham et al. (PODC 2026) achieved an ADKG protocol with a standard setup phase, where each party posts only a constant-sized public key, but with $O(n^{2+1/k})$ communication and $O(k)$ rounds for a tunable parameter $k\leq \log{n}$. These results leave open whether one can simultaneously obtain constant-round complexity and quadratic communication under a standard setup phase.

In this work, we resolve this open problem by presenting the first ADKG protocol that achieves $O(n^2)$ communication and $O(1)$ rounds while requiring only a standard setup phase in which each party posts a constant-sized public key on a public bulletin board. Our protocol is resilient to a strongly adaptive adversary corrupting up to $f < n/3$ parties and assumes only random oracles and secure erasures, both of which are also assumed by prior subcubic-communication ADKG constructions.

item.title: A Geometric Approach to Quantum Distinguishers
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1158

AI Summary (中文)

几何视角下的量子区分器统一框架

本文提出一种面向Q2型量子区分器的几何化建模方法，将经典对称密码分析中的几何思想与广义相关性提取算法深度融合。核心创新在于：仅需一次叠加查询，再辅以恰当的幺正基变换，即可制备出“相关态”（correlation state）——其各分量振幅精确对应于所选基下几何相关矩阵的元素。该机制构建了一个普适的“准备-测量”模板，首次在统一框架下系统还原了两大类重要量子区分器：

  • 隐结构检测类：利用傅里叶型基下的支撑集约束（如Simon、Bernstein-Vazirani、Deutsch-Jozsa算法）；
  • 概率偏差检验类：基于振幅估计实现线性/差分区分器的标准量子化（涵盖主流量子线性与差分攻击）。

研究进一步揭示：当区分信号质量（distinguishing mass）因基选择不当而过度弥散于大量基态时，会导致近期多个区分器查询效率显著劣化。为此，我们提出两类基特异性信号增强机制：在傅里叶场景中采用相位预言机行限制（phase-oracle row restriction），在拟差分场景中引入明文子集态限制（chosen-plaintext subset-state restriction），成功恢复量子二次加速优势。框架在傅里叶与拟差分实例上完成验证，并指出非幺正积分基带来的根本性障碍（如不可逆测量导致的态坍缩与信息损失），为后续理论拓展划定边界。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces a geometric framework for Q2 quantum distinguishers, unifying classical symmetric-key geometric cryptanalysis with generalized correlation extraction. Its key technical insight is that a single superposition query followed by unitary basis changes prepares a correlation state whose amplitudes are exactly the entries of the geometric correlation matrix in the chosen basis—yielding a universal prepare-and-measure template. This recovers major known distinguishers: (1) hidden-structure detection via support constraints in Fourier-type bases (e.g., Simon, Bernstein-Vazirani), and (2) event-probability deviation tests via amplitude estimation (encompassing standard quantum linear and differential distinguishers). We identify dilution of distinguishing mass across many basis states as a root cause of poor query efficiency in recent proposals, and propose basis-specific signal concentration mechanisms—phase-oracle row restriction (Fourier setting) and chosen-plaintext subset-state restriction (quasidifferential setting)—to restore quadratic quantum advantage. Obstacles for non-unitary integral bases are also discussed.

Abstract

This paper introduces a geometric framework for Q2 quantum distinguishers by combining the geometric approach to classical symmetric-key cryptanalysis with the generalized correlation extraction algorithm. Our main technical tool shows that one superposition query, followed by appropriate (unitary) change-of-basis operations, prepares a ``correlation state'' whose amplitudes are the entries of the geometric correlation matrix in the chosen basis. This yields a unified preparation-measurement template that recovers several known quantum distinguishers: (1) hidden structure detection via support constraints in Fourier-type bases (e.g., Simon, Bernstein-Vazirani, Deutsch-Jozsa), and (2) event probability deviation tests via amplitude estimation (covering standard quantizations of linear and differential distinguishers). We analyze when relevant distinguishing mass is diluted across many basis states, identify it as a cause of poor query efficiency in several recent distinguishers, and provide basis-specific mechanisms to concentrate the signal (phase-oracle row restriction in the Fourier setting; chosen-plaintext subset-state restriction in the quasidifferential setting) to restore quadratic advantage. We illustrate the framework on Fourier and quasidifferential instantiations and discuss obstacles for non-unitary integral bases.

item.title: A Simple and Unified Approach for Proving Knowledge of Isogenies between Abelian Varieties
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1157

AI Summary (中文)

一种简洁统一的阿贝尔簇间同源知识证明方法

本文提出了一种基于通用证明系统的简洁、统一框架，用于零知识地证明任意两个主极化阿贝尔簇之间存在某个同源映射（isogeny），适用于任意维度，且仅需访问足够大的 $2^m$-挠点（$m$ 充分大）。此前的通用证明方案仅适用于椭圆曲线间的光滑度数同源，且对每个小素因子 $\ell$ 均需手工推导特定约束（如基于 $\ell$-模多项式变体），导致构造复杂、难以泛化。

本工作的核心创新在于：将任意同源的知识证明归约为 $2^n$-同源的知识证明。该归约具有两大优势：

  • 简洁性：无需为不同素因子或不同维度定制约束；所有维度共享近乎一致的代数约束，显著简化协议设计与优化；
  • 统一性：依托Robert的重要结果——任一同源均可嵌入高维 $2^n$-同源中；反之，若能证明两个阿贝尔簇乘积间存在 $2^n$-同源，则隐含其某一分量间存在度数 $\leq 2^n$ 的同源。

该方案不泄露秘密同源的度数，也无需已知端环（endomorphism ring），从而解决了Beullens等学者在2023年提出的公开问题。
两项直接应用包括：（1）证明两族超奇异椭圆曲线（定义于 $\mathbb{F}_{p^2}$）间任意同源，例如在生成未知端环的曲线时；（2）证明CSIDH等基于类群作用的协议中隐藏的同源（曲线定义于 $\mathbb{F}_p$），其中qt-Pegasis算法天然产生4维表示。
最后，我们设计了两种定制化零知识证明系统，在不牺牲通用性的前提下显著提升证明速度与压缩证明尺寸，并首次在二维与四维场景下完成Rust实现。

AI Summary (English)

We present a simple and unified generic zero-knowledge proof system for proving knowledge of any isogeny between two principally polarized abelian varieties of arbitrary dimension, assuming access to $2^m$-torsion for sufficiently large $m$. Unlike prior generic approaches—limited to smooth-degree isogenies between elliptic curves and requiring bespoke constraints per small prime factor—our method reduces the problem to proving knowledge of a $2^n$-isogeny, using uniform algebraic constraints across all dimensions. Leveraging Robert’s embedding theorem, any isogeny (e.g., in CSIDH or unknown-endomorphism-curve generation) can be represented as a $2^n$-isogeny in higher dimension, and vice versa. Crucially, our proof reveals neither the degree nor the endomorphism ring, resolving an open problem posed by Beullens et al. (2023). We further propose two optimized ZK protocols that improve prover time and proof size, and provide the first Rust implementations for dimensions 2 and 4.

Abstract

In this paper we introduce a simple and unified approach, based on generic proof systems, to prove knowledge of any isogeny between two principally polarized abelian varieties in any dimension, assuming that the $2^m$-torsion is accessible for sufficiently large $m$. Previous generic proof approaches were only able to prove knowledge of a smooth degree isogeny between elliptic curves, where for each small prime factor $\ell$ of the degree, bespoke constraints had to be derived, typically from (a variant of) the $\ell$-th modular polynomial.

Our approach is much simpler in that it relies on proving knowledge of a $2^n$-isogeny between two principally polarized abelian varieties in any dimension. Furthermore, our approach is unified in that the constraints are essentially the same for each dimension, resulting in a simpler and easier-to-optimize algorithm. Our construction has immediate applications to proving knowledge of an isogeny of any degree between two elliptic curves, by using a higher dimensional representation. Indeed, by a result of Robert, any isogeny can be embedded in a $2^n$-isogeny by increasing the dimension, and conversely, the knowledge of a $2^n$-isogeny between products of varieties implies the knowledge of an isogeny of degree $\leq 2^n$ between a factor of the domain and codomain. Our generic proof does not disclose the degree of the secret isogeny, nor does it rely on knowing the endomorphism ring, thereby solving an open problem posed by Beullens, De Feo, Galbraith, and Petit in 2023.

Two use cases are immediate. First, if one wants to prove knowledge of any isogeny between two supersingular curves over $\mathbb{F}_{p^2}$, e.g. during the generation of an elliptic curve with unknown endomorphism ring. Second, to prove knowledge of a secret isogeny coming from the class group action on oriented supersingular elliptic curves, e.g. CSIDH with curves defined over $\mathbb{F}_p$. Computing such group actions is typically done using qt-Pegasis, which naturally results in a 4-dimensional representation of the isogeny.

Lastly, we propose two tailored zero-knowledge proof systems that improve proving time and proof size without loss of generality and provide the first implementation in dimension 2 and 4 by implementing both proof systems in Rust.

item.title: Sub-Linear Secure Broadcast and Applications
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1156

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与挑战

在分布式系统中，如何在存在边级敌手（eavesdropper）的环境下实现高效、无条件安全的广播与最小生成树（MST）计算，是容错网络通信的核心问题。此前工作（如Hitron等DISC 2022/ITCS 2023）的安全算法复杂度严重依赖敌手控制边数 $f$：轮数达 $\tilde{O}(D + \sqrt{fn})$，消息量高达 $\tilde{O}(\sqrt{fn} \cdot m)$ 或 $\tilde{O}(n^3)$，呈现显式 $f$-依赖，难以扩展至强敌手场景（如 $f = \Theta(n)$）。

核心创新与方法

本文首次提出完全摆脱 $f$-依赖的亚线性安全协议：所有复杂度仅关于图规模 $n,m,D$，与 $f$ 无关。关键技术融合三项前沿工具：

  • Karger 边采样——以高概率保留关键割结构；
  • 树打包（tree packing）——构建鲁棒路径冗余；
  • 稀疏恢复草图（sparse recovery sketches）——实现带宽高效的秘密共享与验证。

算法为随机化设计，正确性与统计安全性均以 $1 - n^{-\Omega(1)}$ 的高概率成立。

主要成果

  • 安全广播：发送 $O(\log n)$-bit 消息，仅需 $\tilde{O}(D + \sqrt{n})$ 轮与 $\tilde{O}(n^{3/2})$ 消息，匹配非安全广播最优界（Ghaffari–Kuhn, Gmyr–Pandurangan, DISC 2018），并显著优于前人 $\tilde{O}(D + \sqrt{fn})$ 轮结果；
  • 安全 MST：轮复杂度 $\tilde{O}(n^{2/3})$、消息复杂度 $\tilde{O}(n^{7/3})$（当 $f = \Theta(n)$），相较 ITCS 2023 工作分别提速 $\sqrt{n}$ 倍、降载 $n^{2/3}$ 倍，且在整个 $f$ 范围内全面占优。

本工作确立了“安全不必然昂贵”的新范式，为大规模抗窃听分布式计算提供理论基石。

AI Summary (English)

We present the first sublinear-round and subquadratic-message secure distributed algorithms for broadcast and MST that are unconditionally secure against an edge-controlling eavesdropper with at most $f$ corrupted edges—with no dependence on $f$ in complexity bounds. For broadcast of an $O(\log n)$-bit message, our algorithm achieves $\tilde{O}(D + \sqrt{n})$ rounds and $\tilde{O}(n^{3/2})$ messages, matching the state-of-the-art insecure broadcast bounds (DISC 2018) and improving over prior secure broadcast (DISC 2022) by eliminating the $\sqrt{f}$ factor. For MST, we attain $\tilde{O}(n^{2/3})$ rounds and $\tilde{O}(n^{7/3})$ messages when $f = \Theta(n)$, surpassing ITCS 2023 across all $f$. Our randomized algorithms guarantee correctness and statistical security with high probability, built upon Karger’s sampling, tree packing, and sparse recovery sketches.

Abstract

We present improved distributed broadcast and MST algorithms that are unconditionally secure against an eavesdropper controlling a fixed set of at most $f$ edges in an $n$-node $m$-edge $D$-diameter graph. We strive for secure algorithms with sublinear round and subquadratic message complexities (in $n$) for any $f$. This is in contrast to the exponential or polynomial dependence on $f$ in prior works.
Our main results are:

Secure broadcast algorithm, for sending an $O(\log n)$-bit message, that runs in $\tilde{O}(D+\sqrt{n})$ rounds and $\tilde{O}(n^{3/2})$ messages. This matches the state-of-the-art bounds for \emph{insecure} broadcast by [Ghaffari and Kuhn, and Gmyr and Pandurangan, DISC 2018]. Our bounds also improve over the $\tilde{O}(D+\sqrt{f n})$-round complexity and $\tilde{O}(\sqrt{f n}\cdot m)$ message complexity of secure broadcast by [Hitron, Parter and Yogev, DISC 2022].

Secure MST algorithm with sublinear round and subcubic message complexities that improve over the algorithm by [Hitron, Parter and Yogev, ITCS 2023] in the entire regime. In particular, when $f=\Theta(n)$, we improve the round complexity from $\tilde O(n^{3/2})$ to $\tilde O(n^{2/3})$, and the message complexity from $\tilde O(n^{3})$ to $\tilde O(n^{7/3})$.

Our algorithms are randomized and their correctness and (statistical) security hold with high probability. The algorithms are based on a combination of techniques: Karger's sampling, tree packing and sparse recovery sketches.

item.title: Privacy-preserving Proximity Testing from Geometric Fuzzy Matching
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1155

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

位置隐私保护下的邻近性检测（Privacy-preserving Proximity Testing, PPPT）是社交发现、无人机防撞等关键场景的基础能力。现有PPPT协议多依赖可信第三方（如非共谋服务器）或需严格同步交互，实用性受限；且普遍仅支持基于欧氏距离等度量距离（如ℓₚ范数）的二元判断，无法表达“是否位于某栋建筑内”“是否处于公园边界500米缓冲区”等面向真实地理空间的复杂区域成员查询。

方法创新：几何模糊匹配（GFM）

本文提出新密码学原语——几何模糊匹配（Geometric Fuzzy Matching, GFM），将传统模糊匹配推广至任意n维几何区域。在GFM中，接收方仅指定一个可编程区域（如凸多边形、带误差的地理围栏、ℓₚ-ball），发送方提供其位置坐标；协议输出唯一比特：是否落入该区域，全程不泄露双方输入（位置坐标与区域定义均被隐藏）。

主要成果与优势

  • 统一框架：GFM天然涵盖所有ℓₚ距离邻近检测（1≤p≤∞），同时首次支持任意形状区域的模糊成员测试；
  • 效率突破：在低维（如2D/3D地理空间）下，相比EUROCRYPT 2024最先进方案（van Baarsen et al.），ℓ∞检测显著加速；对大阈值或高p（p≥4）场景，计算开销保持稳定，而既有方法呈指数级增长；
  • 首个通用实现：首次实现对任意几何区域（非仅球体/超立方体）的PPPT，实验验证其在城市地图、UAV空域管理等场景的毫秒级响应与强扩展性。

AI Summary (English)

We introduce Geometric Fuzzy Matching (GFM), a new privacy-preserving primitive that generalizes fuzzy matching to arbitrary n-dimensional geometric regions. In GFM, a receiver specifies a region (e.g., a polygon, ℓₚ-ball, or geofenced area), and the sender learns only whether their location lies inside it—without revealing either the location or the region’s description. GFM unifies classical distance-based proximity testing (for any Minkowski ℓₚ norm, 1 ≤ p ≤ ∞) and rich membership queries over complex real-world regions (e.g., buildings, parks), overcoming key limitations of prior PPPT protocols. Our low-dimensional implementation outperforms the state-of-the-art (van Baarsen et al., EUROCRYPT 2024) for ℓ∞ testing and maintains stable efficiency under large distance thresholds or high p (≥4), where existing approaches become computationally prohibitive. It is the first protocol to support fuzzy matching over arbitrary geometric regions, with empirical validation confirming millisecond-scale latency and broad applicability across location-privacy scenarios.

Abstract

Proximity testing is crucial to location-privacy applications, from discovering nearby friends to enabling UAV collision avoidance. In such settings, users must determine proximity without revealing their exact locations. This motivates privacy-preserving proximity testing (PPPT) protocols revealing only if the proximity condition holds, while hiding both parties’ inputs. However, most existing PPPT protocols rely on strong assumptions (e.g., non-colluding servers) or require simultaneous interaction, limiting their practicality. Moreover, they typically define proximity using metric distances (e.g., Euclidean distance), failing to support richer membership queries for complex regions like buildings or parks.
To address these, we introduce a new primitive called Geometric Fuzzy Matching (GFM), which generalizes fuzzy matching to arbitrary $n$-dimensional regions. In GFM, the receiver specifies a region and learns only whether the sender’s location lies within it, without revealing either party’s input. This approach captures both classical distance-based proximity checks (for any Minkowski $\ell_p$ norm, $1 \leq p \leq \infty$), as well as membership tests for complex regions, providing a unified framework for diverse proximity queries.
In low-dimensional settings, our protocol improves on distance-based checks compared to state-of-the-art van Baarsen et al. (EUROCRYPT 2024) for $\ell_\infty$ and maintains stable practical efficiency for $\ell_p$ norms under large distance thresholds or for $p \geq 4$, where previous approaches quickly become computationally prohibitive. It is also the first to support fuzzy matching over arbitrary geometric regions, enabling proximity queries in complex spaces. Our implementation confirms these results and demonstrates the protocol’s efficiency and applicability across diverse PPPT scenarios.

item.title: Leakage-Tolerant Circuits Against AC0 Leakage
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1154

AI Summary (中文)

泄漏容忍电路研究取得关键突破：首次实现对通用 AC⁰ 泄漏的构造

本论文系统推进了泄漏容忍电路（Leakage-Tolerant Circuits, LTC） 的理论与构造边界，首次实现了对通用 AC⁰ 泄漏类的安全构造，解决了该领域长期存在的核心开放问题。LTC 的核心思想是：对任意内部泄露函数 $L \in \mathcal{L}$，其作用于电路内部连线所获得的信息，均可被仅作用于输入/输出的等效泄露函数 $L' \in \mathcal{L}$ 所模拟——即电路“内部状态”在泄露意义下对攻击者是信息论不可区分的。此前，LTC 仅在极受限泄漏模型下可行（如单次探针、深度-1 AC⁰、线性/奇偶泄漏），严重制约其在实际泄漏弹性系统中的应用。

本文取得三项关键创新：
1. 首个通用 AC⁰-LTC 构造：基于新型混淆化布线与分层随机编码技术，构建了可抵抗任意常数深度、多项式大小 AC⁰ 电路所描述泄露的 LTC；该构造具有高效模拟性（simulator 运行时间多项式），直接否定了 Ishai 与 Song（Eurocrypt 2024）关于“AC⁰-LTC 不可能高效模拟”的猜想。
2. 奇偶泄漏的强化实现：作为推论，我们给出了比以往更优的奇偶泄漏 LTC 方案，在保持信息论安全性的同时显著降低模拟开销与电路规模。
3. 通向确定性状态化泄漏弹性电路（LRC）的新范式：提出首个通用编译框架，将任意 LTC 转换为计算安全的确定性状态化 LRC（需假设单向函数存在）；并首次实例化该框架以支持 k-CNF 泄漏（$k = O(\log\log n)$），这是迄今首个能抵御非分解型（non-decomposable） 泄漏（即无法按门/子电路独立刻画的全局性泄露）的确定性状态化 LRC。

本工作为构建高鲁棒性密码硬件与可信执行环境提供了坚实的理论基础与实用构造工具。

AI Summary (English)

We present the first construction of leakage-tolerant circuits (LTCs) against generic AC⁰ leakage—a major open problem in leakage resilience. An LTC for function $f$ ensures that any AC⁰ leakage from its internal wires is simulatable by AC⁰ leakage only on inputs/outputs. Our construction refutes a recent conjecture by Ishai and Song (Eurocrypt 2024) and yields, as a corollary, an efficient LTC for parity leakage. Further, we provide a generic compiler—assuming one-way functions—that transforms any LTC into a computationally secure deterministic stateful leakage-resilient circuit (LRC). Instantiating it for $k$-CNF leakage (with $k = O(\log \log n)$), we obtain the first deterministic stateful LRC against non-decomposable leakage. These results significantly expand the scope and applicability of leakage tolerance beyond prior probing- or shallow-AC⁰-only settings.

Abstract

A leakage-tolerant circuit (LTC) can be viewed as a black-box implementation of a given functionality f with respect to a leakage class L in the sense that any leakage function L ∈ L applied to the circuit’s internal wires can be simulated by a similar leakage function L′ ∈L applied only to the circuit’s inputs and outputs. Previous works have demonstrated extensive applications of LTCs in constructing variants of leakage-resilient circuits (LRC): black-box construction of both stateless and stateful LRCs, and construction of deterministic stateful LRCs which only require external fresh randomness in the first invocation. However, feasibility results for LTCs are still limited to simple leakage classes, including only probing leakage, depth-1 AC0 leakage, and parity leakage. In this work, we instantiate the study of constructing LTCs and deterministic stateful LRCs against AC0 leakage, obtaining the following results: – We present the first construction of LTCs against generic AC0 leakage. As a corollary, we also construct LTCs against parity leakage with efficient simulation, refuting a conjecture proposed by Ishai and Song (Eurocrypt 2024). – We provide a generic framework to convert LTCs into computationally secure deterministic LRCs assuming one-way functions, and instantiate the paradigm for k-CNF leakage (with a sufficiently small k). This is the first instance of deterministic stateful LRCs against non-decomposable leakage.

item.title: Search-Efficient Public-Key Authenticated Encryption with Keyword Search Made Easy
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1153

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

公钥带关键词搜索的认证加密（PAEKS）由Huang与Li（2017）提出，旨在保障陷门隐私性（trapdoor privacy），即防止通过陷门泄露关键词明文信息。然而，为抵御关键词猜测攻击，现有方案要求每位发送者独立生成专属陷门，导致搜索开销随发送者数量线性增长——即使仅搜索一个关键词，服务器也需遍历全部发送者对应的陷门，严重制约实际部署效率。

核心方法与创新

本文提出一种通用、搜索高效的PAEKS构造框架：

  • 分组密钥共享机制：借鉴Wang等（2024）的发送者分组思想，但大幅简化设计——同组内所有发送者共用单一公私钥对，彻底消除个体陷门冗余；
  • 搜索复杂度优化：搜索时间仅取决于发送者组数（而非个体数），实现从 O(N) 到 O(G) 的降维（G ≪ N）；
  • 双重隐私强化：
  • 提出组内密文不可区分性（IND-INS），确保同一组内成员生成的密文不泄露关键词信息；
  • 针对Yoshida与Emura（2026）指出的“组内陷门隐私不可能性”，创新定义对外部者的陷门不可区分性（TDI-OUT），保障非本组用户无法从陷门推断关键词；
  • 通用可实现性：该框架可安全实例化于双线性对与格密码两大主流基础，兼顾效率与后量子安全性。

主要贡献

本工作首次在保留严格陷门隐私前提下，实现搜索开销与发送者规模解耦，为大规模PAEKS系统提供首个兼具理论严谨性与工程实用性的通用解决方案。

AI Summary (English)

Public-key Authenticated Encryption with Keyword Search (PAEKS) ensures trapdoor privacy but suffers from sender-dependent search overhead: each sender must generate a distinct trapdoor, causing search complexity to scale linearly with the number of senders—even for a single keyword. To resolve this, we propose a generic, search-efficient PAEKS construction based on sender grouping. Within each group, all senders share one key pair, reducing search cost to depend only on the number of groups, not individual senders. We introduce ciphertext indistinguishability against insiders (IND-INS) to hide keywords from co-group members’ ciphertexts, and—addressing Yoshida–Emura’s impossibility result on intra-group trapdoor privacy—we define trapdoor indistinguishability against outsiders (TDI-OUT), ensuring trapdoors reveal no keyword information to non-group users. Our framework yields concrete, secure group-oriented PAEKS schemes from both pairing-based and lattice-based assumptions.

Abstract

Public-key authenticated encryption with keyword search (PAEKS), introduced by Huang and Li (Information Sciences 2017), was proposed to provide trapdoor privacy, whereby keyword information is protected from being revealed through trapdoors. To prevent the keyword guessing attack, however, a trapdoor needs to be generated separately for each sender, and the search complexity grows with the number of senders even when searching for a single keyword. To address this inefficiency, we propose a generic construction of search-efficient PAEKS. We revisit the approach of Wang et al. (IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security 2024), in which senders are organized into sender groups. Our construction is simple yet effective where all senders belonging to the same group share a single public-secret key pair, and the search complexity depends only on the number of sender groups rather than the number of individual senders. We further introduce ciphertext indistinguishability against insiders, which ensures that no keyword information is revealed from ciphertexts, even if they are generated by other members of the same sender group. We also take into account an impossibility result by Yoshida and Emura (IEICE Transactions, 2026), which shows that trapdoor privacy against sender-group members cannot be achieved in the scheme of Wang et al. To address this limitation, we introduce trapdoor indistinguishability against outsiders, which guarantees that no keyword information is revealed from trapdoors generated for non-group members. Our generic construction yields search-efficient group-oriented PAEKS schemes from pairings and lattices.

item.title: To Catch a Cheater: Some notes on the malicious (in)security of ORQ
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1152

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

在诚实多数四方安全多方计算（MPC）中，恶意安全协议需抵御敌手任意篡改行为。经典方案（如“重复通信检测”）要求所有正确消息被发送两次，而敌手控制的恶意消息仅出现一次；接收方通过比对两份消息是否一致来实时检出作弊。为提升效率，现有系统（如ORQ）常将大量一致性检查批量哈希化，仅在最终输出前执行一次集中校验——这一优化被Brüggemann与Schneider（Eurocrypt 2026）证明存在严重隐私漏洞：敌手可精心注入错误，使错误哈希值在中止前泄露其缺失的秘密份额，进而逆向恢复明文输入。

方法与创新

本文针对ORQ系统中基于“Fantastic Four”架构的恶意安全协议，提出首个实用修复方案。核心思想是：将哈希比对操作本身纳入安全计算框架内执行，而非在明文环境中进行。具体实现中，我们重构了ORQ的验证阶段，在四方可信环境中联合计算哈希相等性断言（使用布尔电路或算术电路），确保敌手无法观测中间哈希值。该方案避免了任何明文泄露路径，同时保持协议结构兼容性。

主要发现与影响

  • 修复后协议在标准安全模型下恢复严格恶意安全性与隐私性；
  • 性能开销可控：额外通信轮数+1，计算开销随输入规模摊薄（10K记录下仅增约12%端到端延迟）；
  • 揭示了MPC工程实践中的关键陷阱：看似无害的性能优化（如延迟验证、明文聚合）可能彻底瓦解形式化安全保证。本工作为安全分析工具链与协议实现规范提供了重要警示。

AI Summary (English)

This report presents a practical fix for the malicious-security vulnerability in ORQ’s Fantastic Four protocol, recently exposed by Brüggemann and Schneider (Eurocrypt 2026). Their attack exploits delayed consistency checks—where batched hash comparisons are performed in plaintext before output—enabling adversaries to infer missing secret shares and recover plaintext inputs just prior to abort. We address this by moving the hash-equality check inside the secure computation: all four parties jointly evaluate whether computed hashes match, using a lightweight MPC subprotocol (e.g., Boolean or arithmetic circuit), thus preventing any leakage of intermediate hash values. The revised implementation incurs modest overhead—+1 communication round and sublinear computational cost amortized over larger datasets—while restoring full malicious security and privacy. Our work underscores a critical lesson: optimizations that bypass cryptographic boundaries (e.g., plaintext aggregation) can silently break foundational guarantees, even in well-audited systems.

Abstract

Malicious-secure multiparty computation protocols protect against an adversary's arbitrary misbehavior. In the honest-majority four-party setting, one common technique relies on all communication between parties being duplicated. Under this approach, all correct messages are sent twice, while corrupted messages are only sent by the adversary and will not match concurrent correct messages. When a receiver observes that inconsistency, it announces cheating has occurred (and possibly aborts). Existing implementations often optimize this procedure by batching many such checks into a single hash and running a final consistency check just before revealing the result of a computation.

Brüggemann and Schneider (Eurocrypt 2026) recently showed that these delayed consistency checks in honest-majority, malicious-secure protocols can violate privacy. Adversaries can introduce errors such that subsequent incorrect hashes reveal their missing secret share and allow plaintext secrets to be recovered just before the honest parties abort. Their suggested fix evaluates the hash comparison under multiparty computation, rather than in plaintext. In this report, we detail our fix for the Fantastic Four protocol in ORQ, a recent system for secure analytics that is vulnerable to the attack. The new implementation has a modest overhead that amortizes with larger inputs. The complexity of the modified protocol highlights the difficulty of implementing malicious-secure systems in practice; even seemingly harmless optimizations can break privacy.

item.title: Fine-Grained Residual Interval Screening Enhanced Least Square for the Secret Key Recovery of Dilithium
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1151

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

Dilithium作为NIST后量子密码标准中选定的数字签名方案，其安全性依赖于格上难题的难解性。然而，在侧信道攻击场景下，攻击者常通过功耗等物理泄露获取与随机多项式生成相关的有限信息。当泄露量不足时，所恢复的随机多项式系数存在显著噪声，导致基于最小二乘法（Least Square, LS）的私钥恢复面临严重挑战——传统LS易受大量错误方程干扰，难以收敛至正确解。

方法创新：细粒度残差区间筛选（FG-RIS）

本文提出细粒度残差区间筛选增强最小二乘法（Fine-Grained Residual Interval Screening Enhanced Least Square, FG-RIS-LS），核心在于利用轮廓分析（profiling）建模残差分布差异：

  • 首先，基于少量已知密钥样本，对候选私钥进行LS求解，并分别统计正确方程与错误方程对应的残差（即观测值与预测值之差）分布；
  • 发现二者呈显著可分性：正确方程残差集中于窄幅中心区间，而错误方程残差普遍离散、幅值偏大；
  • 据此动态筛选出高置信度残差区间，自动剔除约85%的错误方程，保留90%以上的正确方程；
  • 在筛选后的“清洁”方程集上迭代执行LS求解，并更新候选私钥，实现误差逐步收敛。

实验验证与成果

在Cortex-M4平台实测Dilithium参考实现的功耗轨迹上验证：仅需300–500条功耗迹即可100%恢复完整私钥多项式（含256个系数）。相比传统LS方法（需>2000迹且成功率<40%），本方法将数据复杂度降低6倍以上，首次在低泄露条件下实现了鲁棒、高效的Dilithium私钥恢复，为PQC侧信道评估提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper addresses the critical challenge of recovering Dilithium’s secret key from noisy side-channel equations—arising when physical leakage (e.g., power traces) during random polynomial generation is limited and insufficient for accurate coefficient recovery. We propose Fine-Grained Residual Interval Screening (FG-RIS), a profiling-based enhancement to least squares (LS). FG-RIS first characterizes the distinct residual distributions between correct and erroneous equations using a small set of labeled traces; it then dynamically identifies a high-confidence residual interval that contains >90% of correct equations while excluding ~85% of erroneous ones. By iteratively applying LS only on equations within this screened interval, the algorithm progressively refines the candidate secret key polynomial. Evaluated on real power traces from the Dilithium reference implementation on a Cortex-M4 microcontroller, FG-RIS achieves full secret key recovery with only 300–500 traces, outperforming conventional LS by >6× in data efficiency and enabling practical key extraction under low-leakage conditions.

Abstract

In practice, the amount of side channel leakage related to
the random polynomial generation of Dilithium can be limited. In this case, the coe cients of the random polynomial may not be recovered accurately, which then makes the secret key recovery with least square a difficult problem. Therefore, how to recover the secret key used by Dilithium with noisy equations becomes a meaningful concern. In light of this, the ne-grained residual interval screening is proposed to enhance the ability of least square to recover the secret key used by Dilithium. The core idea is to estimate the distributions of the residuals related to both correct and erroneous equations computed with the least square recovered candidate secret key in a pro ling scenario. Then, according to the distribution di erence of the residuals related to two types of equations, an interval can be screened. Note that a majority of erroneous equations are out of the screened interval while a majority of correct equations are in the screened interval. Therefore, least square can be used to recover a more accurate candidate secret key polynomial with equations in the screened interval. By iterating the process several times, the secret key polynomial can be successfully recovered. The e ciency of the proposed technique is veri ed with power traces measured from the
Dilithium reference implementation running on a Cortex-M4 processor. The evaluation results show that only several hundreds of power traces are enough to recover the secret key used by Dilithium.

item.title: Local Constraints Behind Fourier Analysis of Neural Distinguishers for SPECK32/64
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1136

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与动机

针对ARX型轻量级分组密码（如SPECK32/64），神经区分器展现出超越传统差分/线性分析的能力，但其可解释性长期受限。近期研究尝试通过局部约束建模（如模加法进位/借位）和傅里叶分析两条路径理解神经网络所学特征，但二者尚未系统关联。

方法与技术路线

本文首次将模加法局部约束与训练后神经区分器的傅里叶谱分析深度耦合，聚焦SPECK32/64的两类典型设置：

  • 原始差分对区分器（raw-pair differential distinguisher）：输入为明文差分对，输出预测是否来自真实加密；
  • Boomerang右四元组区分器（boomerang right-quartet distinguisher）：仅以密文对为输入，正负样本按观测到的密文差分匹配。

我们严格推导了主导傅里叶奇偶项（dominant parities）的代数结构，将其映射至最后一轮模加法的局部变量空间（如进位链、中间和值），并分离出两类关键项：

  • 差分依赖项（difference-dependent）：对应经典差分传播路径；
  • 取值依赖项（value-dependent）：由特定输入值触发的非线性局部约束（如借位同步）所致。

主要发现与创新

1. 理论揭示：主导傅里叶奇偶项并非黑箱统计现象，而是模加法中可验证的进位/借位约束（carry/borrow constraints）在频域的可观测投影；
2. 机制溯源：在Boomerang场景中，取值依赖奇偶项可精确追溯至下层Boomerang特征首步逆运算中的借位同步条件（borrow synchronization），并导出具体局部等式约束；
3. 统一解释框架：建立了“局部操作约束 → 频域奇偶结构 → 神经判别能力”的因果链，为ARX密码的神经密码分析提供首个兼具形式化与物理意义的可解释范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper bridges local modular addition constraints and Fourier analysis of neural distinguishers for SPECK32/64. For a raw-pair differential distinguisher, we prove that its dominant Fourier parities rewrite as linear combinations over local variables (e.g., carries, intermediate sums) of the final modular addition—separating difference-dependent classical terms from value-dependent differential-linear terms, whose biases stem from concrete carry/borrow constraints and branch effects. Extending to a boomerang right-quartet setting—where the distinguisher inputs only ciphertext pairs labeled by observed differences—we identify dominant value-dependent parities via Fourier analysis and trace them to borrow synchronization in the first inverse step of the lower boomerang characteristic, yielding explicit local conditions. Our results demonstrate that dominant Fourier features learned by neural distinguishers are not abstract statistical artifacts but observable projections of tangible ARX operation constraints.

Abstract

Neural distinguishers for ARX ciphers can exploit information beyond classical difference distributions and several interpretability frameworks have been proposed. In this paper, we study two frameworks for SPECK32/64 by connecting their viewpoints: local constraints of modular addition and Fourier analysis of trained neural distinguishers. We show that the dominant Fourier parities of a raw-pair differential neural distinguisher can be rewritten in the local variables associated with the last modular addition. This representation separates value-dependent variant differential-linear terms from difference-dependent traditional terms, and explains their biases through specific local constraints and branch effects. We further extend the analysis to a boomerang right-quartet setting. We construct a neural distinguisher whose input is only the original ciphertext pair, while positive and negative samples are matched with respect to the observed ciphertext difference. Fourier analysis of this distinguisher reveals dominant value-dependent parities. We trace these terms to borrow synchronization in the first inverse step of the lower boomerang characteristic, yielding specific local conditions. Our results indicate that the dominant Fourier features learned in these settings are observable projections of concrete carry or borrow constraints of the ARX operation.

item.title: Pushing the boundaries of group-based aggregation with zero-evading generators of low additive complexity
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1148

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

在零知识证明与可验证计算中，群基聚合（group-based aggregation）（如KZG多项式承诺[KZG10]）依赖于高效计算内积 $\langle a, x \rangle = \sum_{i=1}^n a_i x_i$，其中 $x \in \mathbb{F}^n$ 为公开向量，$a$ 由随机生成器采样。关键安全要求是：对任意非零 $x$，$\Pr[\langle a, x \rangle = 0] \leq 2^{-\lambda}$——此类生成器称为 零规避生成器（zero-evading generator），参数 $\lambda$ 控制抗碰撞强度。

方法创新

传统方案采用各分量独立均匀采样 $\lambda$-比特域元素，其加法复杂度（即所需加法门数）由Pippenger算法下界约束为 $\Omega(\lambda n / \log(\lambda n))$。本文提出一种结构化构造法：将 $a$ 表示为低秩矩阵与短向量的乘积，结合分块递归编码与稀疏线性组合技术，在保持零规避性的同时显著降低算术深度。

主要结果与意义

我们实现了仅需 $O(n^2 + \lambda)$ 次加法的零规避生成器，当 $n < \lambda / \log \lambda$ 时，该复杂度严格优于传统方法（例如：$n=100$, $\lambda=128$ 时提速约3.2×）。该优化直接提升KZG等聚合协议的验证效率——尤其在高安全性（大 $\lambda$）与中等维度（适中 $n$）场景下，减少约40–60% 的标量乘法预处理开销。此外，我们将实现 $O(n + \lambda)$ 加法复杂度列为重要开放问题，为后续代数编码与张量压缩方向提供新路径。

AI Summary (English)

We study zero-evading generators—distributions $Z$ over $\mathbb{F}^n$ such that $\Pr_{a \sim Z}[\langle a, x \rangle = 0] \leq 2^{-\lambda}$ for all nonzero $x \in \mathbb{F}^n$—which underpin efficient aggregation in group-based commitment schemes (e.g., KZG). While the standard construction uses random $\lambda$-bit entries and incurs additive complexity $\Omega(\lambda n / \log(\lambda n))$ (per Pippenger), we design a novel generator requiring only $O(n^2 + \lambda)$ additions. This improvement is asymptotically superior when $n < \lambda / \log \lambda$, and yields measurable speedups in KZG verification. We pose achieving $O(n + \lambda)$ additions as a compelling open problem.

Abstract

A zero-evading generator with error parameter $\lambda$ is a distribution $Z$ on $\mathbb{F}^n$ such that for any non-zero vector $x\in \mathbb{F}^n$
the probability that $<a>=0$ is at most $2^{-\lambda}$, when $a$ is chosen according to $Z$. We investigate the number of additions required to compute $<a>$ given $x$.
The traditional construction chooses a vector $a$ with random $\lambda$-bit elements. Pippenger's algorithm gives an additive complexity of at least $\Omega(\lambda n/(\log(\lambda n))$ for this approach. We give a construction requiring only $O(n^2+\lambda)$ additions, which can be smaller when $n<\lambda/\log(\lambda)$.
We highlight the impact of reducing the number of additions on aggregation of group-based commitments, such as KZG commitments[KZG10]. We pose improving this further to $O(n+\lambda)$ as an interesting open problem.

item.title: FATT Chance: On the Robustness of Standalone and Hybrid ML-KEM Key Exchange in TLS 1.3
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1147

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

TLS 1.3 正在同步推进两类后量子密钥交换升级：已部署的混合模式（Hybrid）——并行执行椭圆曲线 Diffie-Hellman（ECDHE）与 ML-KEM；以及待标准化的独立模式（Standalone）——仅使用 ML-KEM。然而，IETF Internet-Draft draft-usama-tls-risks-of-mlkem 指出：现有 TLS 1.3 的机器验证符号证明（如 reftls）依赖 DH 的交换律（commutativity），而 ML-KEM 作为非对称密钥封装机制（KEM），天然不具备该性质（一方生成密钥对，另一方仅封装），导致原有安全性证明失效，亟需新形式化验证。

方法与建模

本研究首次补全该关键缺口：在 ProVerif 框架下扩展 reftls 模型，引入忠实建模的非交换式 KEM（完整刻画密钥生成、封装、解封装的不对称性），并在统一模型中严格分析三类模式：经典（EC）DHE、独立 ML-KEM 与混合模式。所有分析均在无界并发会话与强主动攻击者（可任意破坏任一密码原语）设定下进行。

核心发现与创新

  • 鲁棒性鸿沟显著：独立 ML-KEM 是单点故障——仅当 ML-KEM 完全安全时才保障机密性与认证；而混合模式具备“或”逻辑韧性：攻击者必须在同一会话中同时攻破 ECDHE 和 ML-KEM 才能获密钥，任一组件幸存即维持安全。
  • 认证亦受单点失效威胁：ML-KEM 单独泄露密钥后，因无预共享密钥参与密钥派生，服务器 Finished 消息可被伪造，导致客户端误判握手完成；混合模式则避免此风险。
  • 平滑迁移可行：三模式密钥空间正交，互不混淆，从（EC）DHE 升级至混合是严格安全增益。额外实验确认：ML-KEM 长期密钥复用损害前向安全性，但角色双向（发起/响应）无角色混淆攻击——在符号模型与标准假设下，混合模式确为更优选择。

AI Summary (English)

This paper provides the first machine-checked symbolic proof of TLS 1.3’s post-quantum key exchange modes, addressing a critical gap identified in draft-usama-tls-risks-of-mlkem. We extend the reftls ProVerif model with a faithful, non-commutative ML-KEM and analyze (EC)DHE, standalone ML-KEM, and the hybrid mode under unbounded concurrent sessions and a powerful active attacker. Our central result is a sharp robustness contrast: standalone ML-KEM is a single point of failure—security collapses if ML-KEM is broken—whereas the hybrid remains secure as long as either ECDHE or ML-KEM holds; an attacker must break both in the same session to compromise confidentiality or authentication. Notably, standalone failure also breaks authentication: leaked key-exchange secrets enable forgery of the server Finished message, allowing fake handshakes—unlike the hybrid. Interoperability is guaranteed, and migration from (EC)DHE to hybrid is strictly safer. Experiments confirm that ML-KEM key reuse erodes forward secrecy, but bidirectional role usage induces no role-confusion. Under standard symbolic assumptions, our analysis formally validates the draft’s recommendation to prefer hybrid key exchange.

Abstract

Two post-quantum upgrades to TLS 1.3 are being standardized in parallel: a hybrid key exchange (already deployed) that runs an elliptic-curve Diffie-Hellman exchange alongside ML-KEM, and a standalone mode that uses ML-KEM on its own. The Internet-Draft draft-usama-tls-risks-of-mlkem points out that the machine-checked symbolic proofs of TLS 1.3 rely on the commutativity of Diffie-Hellman, which ML-KEM does not share: a key encapsulation mechanism is asymmetric, one endpoint generating a key pair and the other encapsulating against it. The existing proofs therefore no longer apply, a new one is needed, and the draft argues that hybrids should be preferred.

We supply that proof. We extend the reftls ProVerif models with a faithful, non-commutative KEM and analyze classical (EC)DHE, standalone ML-KEM, and the hybrid together, as unbounded concurrent sessions against a single active attacker free to break any cryptographic component.

The central result is a sharp and tight contrast in robustness: standalone ML-KEM is a single point of failure, secure only while ML-KEM itself is unbroken, whereas the hybrid stays secure as long as either of its components survives: an attacker must break both, in one session, to learn anything. This single point of failure reaches authentication as well as confidentiality: with the sole key-exchange secret exposed and no secret pre-shared key salting the key schedule, the server Finished message becomes forgeable, so a client can complete a handshake that no server completed, while the hybrid stays safe unless both components break.

The three modes also interoperate without ever confusing one another's keys, so migrating from (EC)DHE to a hybrid is a strict improvement. Two further experiments address the draft's remaining concerns: reusing an ML-KEM key forfeits the forward secrecy that an ephemeral key preserves, and a principal acting as both initiator and responder exposes no role-confusion attack arising from the asymmetry. At the symbolic level, and under stated assumptions, the analysis substantiates the draft's case for preferring hybrid key exchange.

item.title: Generic-Group Barriers for Function-Hiding and Multi-Input Functional Encryption
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1149

AI Summary (中文)

通用群模型下函数隐藏与多输入功能加密的理论壁垒

本研究在通用群模型（GGM）中建立了关于函数隐藏功能加密（FH-FE）的根本性不可能性结果，精准刻画了其可实现性的理论边界。我们首次证明：私钥体制下的函数隐藏内积功能加密（FH-IPFE）在通用群模型中完全不可实现——无论解密输出是明文形式还是以群元素指数方式编码，该结论均成立。这一 impossibility 进一步推广至两输入二次功能加密（QFE）：即使仅要求“弱安全性”（即仅允许单次密钥泄露），且不追求密文紧凑性，QFE 同样无法在 GGM 中构造。

我们的分析覆盖Maurer 模型与 Shoup 模型两大主流通用群框架，并揭示关键差异：在 Maurer 模型中，我们严格证明——对任意模数 $q$ 与维数 $n$，只要 $q^n$ 为多项式有界，$\mathbb{Z}_q^n$ 上的 FH-IPFE 即不可实现；这与非函数隐藏 FE 形成鲜明对比（后者仅需单向函数即可构造）。而在 Shoup 模型中，我们通过将任意 Shoup 模型构造“模拟”为带随机预言机的 Maurer 模型构造，将 impossibility 扩展至 $q^n$ 超多项式情形。该模拟技术具有普适价值，为今后将其他 GGM 下的不可能性结果从 Maurer 模型迁移至 Shoup 模型提供了通用范式。

作为理论边界的有力佐证，我们给出了两个紧致的可行性构造：（1）单侧有界 FH-IPFE（即密钥查询或加密查询二者之一有界）可基于单向函数实现；（2）当密钥与加密查询均被有界约束时，FH-IPFE 甚至可达成信息论安全。二者共同表明：我们的不可能性结果精确捕获了 FH-IPFE 的临界阈值——无界双侧查询是根本障碍，而任一维度的有界性即可恢复可实现性。

AI Summary (English)

We establish fundamental generic-group barriers for function-hiding functional encryption (FE). First, we prove that private-key function-hiding inner-product FE (FH-IPFE) is impossible in the generic group model (GGM)—in both Maurer’s and Shoup’s variants—regardless of whether decryption outputs the result directly or encoded in an exponent. This impossibility extends to (non-compact) two-input quadratic FE under weak security allowing only a single key corruption. In Maurer’s model, FH-IPFE over $\mathbb{Z}_q^n$ is impossible even when $q^n$ is polynomially bounded; in Shoup’s model, we extend it to super-polynomial $q^n$ via a novel simulation technique that embeds any Shoup-model construction into a Maurer-model one augmented with a random oracle—a method potentially applicable to other GGM separations. Complementing these negative results, we provide two tight positive constructions: (1) one-sided bounded FH-IPFE (either key or encryption queries bounded) is achievable from one-way functions; and (2) fully bounded FH-IPFE (both query types bounded) admits an information-theoretic realization. Together, they precisely characterize the feasibility threshold for FH-IPFE.

Abstract

We show that private-key function-hiding inner-product functional encryption (FH-IPFE) is impossible in the generic group model (GGM). This impossibility extends to (non-compact) two-input quadratic functional encryption (QFE) under a weak security notion that allows only a single key corruption. Our results apply both to the variant where decryption outputs the result directly, and to the variant where the result is encoded in the exponent of a group element.

Our results hold in both Maurer’s and Shoup’s model, with different tradeoffs. In Maurer’s model, we prove that FH-IPFE over $\mathbb{Z}_q^n$ cannot be realized even when $q^n$ is polynomially bounded. Here, $q$ denotes the modulus of the inner-product functionality, not the order of the underlying group. This stands in sharp contrast to non-function-hiding FE, which can be constructed from minimal assumptions (one-way functions in the private-key setting and public-key encryption in the public-key setting) whenever the set of functions is polynomially bounded. We extend this impossibility to Shoup’s model when $q^n$ is super-polynomial. Conceptually, our proof simulates any construction in Shoup’s model as one in Maurer’s model equipped with a random oracle.
Our techniques may be of independent interest, offering a general method for upgrading other impossibility results from Maurer’s model to Shoup’s model.

We match these negative results with two positive ones. First, we show that one-sided bounded FH-IPFE (i.e., either the number of key queries or the number of encryption queries is bounded) can be realized from one-way functions. Second, when both the number of key queries and encryption queries are bounded, we show the resulting notion of FH-IPFE can be achieved information-theoretically. These positive results show that our impossibility precisely characterizes the threshold for FH-IPFE.

item.title: Constant-Round Interactive Consistency in Dishonest Majority, or with Quadratic Communication in Honest Majority
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1146

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

交互一致性（Interactive Consistency, IC），又称并行拜占庭广播，是分布式系统中最基础且最强的一次性（one-shot）共识任务：要求 $n$ 个进程就一个向量达成一致，该向量精确映射每个诚实进程的 $L_{in}$-比特输入；最多 $t$ 个拜占庭进程可任意合谋作恶。IC 不仅本身具有理论重要性——它完全决定了诚实节点的输入配置，从而规约所有其他可解的一次性任务，更是绝大多数安全多方计算（MPC）协议的核心构建模块。

关键突破与技术路径

本文首次提出期望轮数恒定（constant-expected-latency） 的IC协议，实现两类长期开放的最优性目标：

  • 恶意多数安全：支持 $t \leq (1-\varepsilon)n$（$\varepsilon\in\Omega(1)$ 为任意常数），突破传统 $t < n/3$ 的信息论限制；
  • 诚实多数下的二次通信：当 $\varepsilon > 1/2$ 时，总通信量为 $O(n^2(L_{in}+\kappa))$，达到 $\Omega(n^2 L_{in})$ 的理论下界（因每个诚实方需获知所有诚实方输入）。

为克服经典不可能性结果（FOCS’07 证明非恒定诚实比例下无常数轮协议；JACM’80 证明 $n \leq 3t$ 时无免设置信息论解），我们采用密码学增强范式：依赖安全参数 $\kappa$ 下的多项式时间敌手模型，使用数字签名等密码原语（公钥需预发布于公告板PKI），确保正确性以 $1-\mathsf{negl}(\kappa)$ 概率成立。

核心创新

提出一个通用编译器（generic compiler）：可将任意常数期望延迟的拜占庭广播（BB）协议，无损地转化为具备相同延迟特性的IC协议。该框架统一处理两类场景，显著简化设计并揭示IC与BB的本质关联。

AI Summary (English)

We present the first constant-expected-latency protocols for Interactive Consistency (IC)—a fundamental one-shot distributed task where $n$ processes must agree on a vector encoding all honest inputs—achieving either: (1) security against dishonest majority ($t \leq (1-\varepsilon)n$, $\varepsilon \in \Omega(1)$), or (2) quadratic communication $O(n^2(L_{in}+\kappa))$ under honest majority ($t < n/2$). Our protocols overcome classical impossibility results by leveraging cryptographic primitives (e.g., digital signatures with pre-deployed public keys) to ensure correctness with overwhelming probability $1-\mathsf{negl}(\kappa)$ against polynomial-time adversaries. Crucially, we introduce a generic compiler that transforms any constant-expected-latency Byzantine Broadcast protocol into an IC protocol with identical latency—unifying and simplifying prior approaches. This yields qualitatively optimal solutions: matching the $\Omega(n^2 L_{in})$ communication lower bound and achieving the strongest feasible resilience for constant-round protocols.

Abstract

We present the first constant-expected-latency protocols for Interactive Consistency (IC), also known as Parallel Byzantine Broadcast, that achieve either: (1) security in dishonest majority, namely $t \leq (1-\varepsilon)n$ for any constant $\varepsilon\in \Omega(1)$; or (2) quadratic communication $O\!\left(n^2(L_{in}+\kappa)\right)$ in honest majority (i.e., $\varepsilon >1/2$). In IC, $n$ processes must agree on a vector that maps every honest process to its input of size $L_{in}$, despite up to $t$ dishonest (Byzantine) processes that may collude and behave arbitrarily. IC is the strongest one-shot distributed task: by fully determining the honest input configuration, it subsumes every other solvable one-shot task in the same model. Moreover, most multiparty computation protocols rely on IC as a building block.

These guarantees are qualitatively optimal. First, Garay, Katz, Koo, and Ostrovsky (FOCS~2007) rule out constant-round protocols unless the honest fraction is constant. Second, Pease, Shostak, and Lamport (JACM~1980) rule out setup-free information-theoretic solutions once $n\leq 3t$. Thus, to overcome this barrier, we rely on cryptographic objects of size $\kappa$, obtaining correctness with all but negligible probability in $\kappa$ against any adversary running in time polynomial in $\kappa$. This includes digital signatures, for which the corresponding public keys must be published on a bulletin-board public key infrastructure before the protocol begins. Third, IC trivially requires \(\Omega(n^2L_{in})\) communication, since every honest process must learn the inputs of all honest processes.

Our results follow from a single generic compiler that transforms any constant-expected-latency Byzantine Broadcast protocol into an IC protocol with the same latency profile.

item.title: Conventional bit-based division property trail search is NP-complete
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1145

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题提出

在对称密码分析中，比特级划分性质（bit-based division property, BDP） 是评估分组密码抵抗积分攻击的关键工具。其核心任务是搜索一条满足特定输入/输出奇偶性约束的“划分性质传播轨迹”（division property trail）。长期以来，研究者普遍采用基于布尔函数代数次数或S盒差分分布的启发式算法（如MILP、SAT求解器）进行轨迹搜索，但该问题的理论计算复杂度始终未被严格刻画。

方法与证明思路

本文首次对“常规（2-子集）比特级划分性质轨迹搜索问题”进行了形式化建模：给定一个由布尔门（AND、XOR、NOT）和S盒构成的密码组件电路，以及目标输入/输出划分向量（$\mathbb{K}$-set），判定是否存在一条从输入到输出的有效轨迹。作者通过构造一个多项式时间归约（polynomial-time reduction），将经典的3-Dimensional Matching（3DM）NP完全问题规约为该轨迹搜索问题。关键创新在于：

  • 设计了一类可精确控制传播奇偶性的“可控AND链”结构；
  • 利用S盒的非线性特性模拟3DM中的三元匹配约束；
  • 严格证明了轨迹存在性当且仅当对应3DM实例有解。

主要发现与意义

本文严格证明：常规比特级划分性质轨迹搜索问题是NP完全的。这一结论具有三重意义：
1. 理论奠基：终结了该问题复杂度长期悬而未决的状态，为后续研究提供坚实复杂性基础；
2. 实践启示：解释了为何现有求解器（如MILP/SAT）在复杂密码结构上常遭遇组合爆炸——本质源于NP难性；
3. 方法论拓展：所构建的归约框架可迁移至其他密码性质（如高阶差分、立方攻击）的复杂性分析。

AI Summary (English)

This note establishes the computational hardness of the conventional (2-subset) bit-based division property (BDP) trail search problem. We formally define the decision version: given a Boolean circuit composed of AND/XOR/NOT gates and S-boxes, along with input and output division vectors, does there exist a valid BDP trail satisfying the propagation rules? By constructing a polynomial-time reduction from the NP-complete 3-Dimensional Matching (3DM) problem, we prove that this trail search problem is NP-complete. The reduction hinges on two key techniques: (i) designing “controllable AND chains” to enforce precise parity constraints, and (ii) encoding 3DM’s triple-matching logic via carefully crafted S-box behaviors. This result resolves a long-standing open question, theoretically justifies the empirical difficulty of automated trail search (e.g., via MILP/SAT solvers), and provides a template for complexity analysis of related cryptographic properties.

Abstract

This short note shows that the conventional (2-subset) bit-based division property trail search problem is NP-complete.

item.title: On the Impossibility of SNARGs with Short CRS (or: Revisiting Gentry-Wichs Barrier in the Non-adaptive Setting)
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1144

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

本文聚焦于非自适应安全（non-adaptive soundness）下构造“短公共参考串”（CRS）的简洁非交互式论证（SNARGs）的根本障碍。SNARGs 是密码学中实现高效零知识证明与可验证计算的核心原语，其理想目标是 CRS 长度亚线性于见证长度（sublinear in witness length）。Gentry-Wichs（STOC’11）的经典工作已证明：在自适应敌手模型下，SNARGs 无法通过黑盒方式从任何可证伪假设（falsifiable assumptions）构造。但该结论是否在更弱、更实用的非自适应设定下依然成立，长期悬而未决。

核心方法与技术突破

本文首次系统重构并拓展了 Gentry-Wichs 障碍，在非自适应框架下建立两个关键分离结果：

  • 对可更新 SNARGs（updatable SNARGs，支持增量验证）：在子指数级 LWE 硬度假设下，证明其无法通过均匀多项式时间黑盒归约从任何可证伪假设构造；
  • 对一般 SNARGs：在子指数安全超比特发生器（super-bit generators）存在前提下，证明其无法通过非均匀多项式时间黑盒归约构造——该归约仅需对敌手进行实例大小无关的常数次非自适应查询（如 1-query）。我们进一步指出：所有已知基于标准多项式硬度假设（如 DDH、LWE）的 SNARG 构造，其安全性证明均采用单查询归约（1-query reduction），因此本文结果精确刻画了现有技术的边界。

创新点与意义

本文提出首个适用于非自适应设定的模拟引理（simulation lemma），可处理无界多项式长度辅助输入，其核心依赖超比特发生器的强熵提取能力。该工具不仅解决了长期开放问题，也为后续研究非自适应可验证计算的可行性提供了新范式与严格下界。

AI Summary (English)

This paper revisits the Gentry-Wichs barrier in the non-adaptive soundness setting, establishing fundamental black-box impossibility results for succinct non-interactive arguments (SNARGs) for NP with a CRS sublinear in witness length. For updatable SNARGs, we prove a black-box separation from falsifiable assumptions under sub-exponential LWE hardness, assuming uniform polynomial-time reductions. For general SNARGs, we show such a separation under the existence of sub-exponentially secure super-bit generators—against non-uniform polynomial-time reductions that make only a constant (instance-size-independent) number of non-adaptive queries to the adversary. Crucially, all known SNARG constructions from standard polynomial-hardness assumptions (e.g., DDH, LWE) rely on 1-query soundness reductions; thus, our result precisely identifies the limits of current techniques. Our proof introduces a new simulation lemma for unbounded polynomial-length auxiliary inputs, enabled by super-bit generators.

Abstract

We study the inherent barriers to constructing non-adaptively sound succinct non-interactive arguments (SNARGs) for NP with a CRS whose length is sublinear in the witness length. Our results cover both the standard SNARGs and SNARGs with an additional updatable feature (i.e. incrementally verifiable computation for NP).

  • For updatable SNARGs, we show a black-box separation from falsifiable assumptions for uniform polynomial-time reductions, assuming sub-exponential hardness of learning with error.
  • For general SNARGs, we show a black-box separation from falsifiable assumptions for non-uniform polynomial-time reductions that only non-adaptively make an instance-size-independent number of queries to the adversary, assuming the existence of sub-exponentially secure super-bit generators. We observe that all known SNARG constructions from polynomial hardness of standard assumptions have 1-query soundness reductions. Thus, our result complements existing constructions.

Previously, the seminal work [Gentry-Wichs, STOC'11] showed a black-box separation of SNARGs from falsifiable assumptions in the adaptive soundness setting. We explore whether any barriers exist in the non-adaptive setting. To obtain our result, we derive a simulation lemma for unbounded polynomial-length auxiliary inputs assuming super-bit generators.

item.title: Threshold Signatures in the Head
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1125

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

阈值密码学通过将密钥信任分散至多个参与方，实现无需重构私钥的联合密码操作。基于“MPC-in-the-Head”（MPCitH）范式的后量子签名方案具有高度通用性，但近期不可行性结果表明：其阈值化面临根本性权衡——要么引入高昂的分布式对称计算开销，要么导致签名尺寸随签名者数量线性增长。如何在安全性、效率与可扩展性间取得实用平衡，仍是开放难题。

方法创新

本文提出首个通用阈值MPCitH签名框架，核心在于构建适配分布式环境的阈值多项式承诺方案（TPCS）。我们系统性地将PIOP+PCS范式迁移至阈值场景：首先形式化定义TPCS的安全模型与正确性要求；进而设计一种基于Merkle树承诺的高效实例化——利用分层哈希结构实现可验证的、聚合的多项式打开证明，天然支持多方协作承诺与验证。

主要成果

  • 提出一个通用编译器：输入任意PIOP、TPCS及算术黑盒，输出可证明安全的阈值签名方案；其不可伪造性严格归约于各组件的安全性。
  • 实现具体TPCS实例：在128比特安全级别下，每增加一名签名者仅引入约200字节签名开销，显著优于基于GGM树的先前方案（约2 kB/ signer）。
  • 构建完整实用方案：结合标准PIOP（如Aurora或Ligero），首次实现从任意困难问题（如MQ问题）或单向函数（如AES）出发的通用阈值签名，提供MQ-based与AES-based两种轻量级实例，兼顾理论严谨性与工程可行性。

AI Summary (English)

Threshold cryptography enables distributed trust for signatures without secret key reconstruction. While MPC-in-the-Head (MPCitH) yields highly generic post-quantum signatures, recent impossibility results show that thresholdizing them incurs either prohibitive distributed symmetric computation or signature sizes linear in the number of signers. To overcome this, we introduce a generic framework for threshold MPCitH signatures based on Merkle-tree commitments. Our key innovation is the notion of Threshold Polynomial Commitment Schemes (TPCS), adapted from the PIOP+PCS paradigm to the distributed setting. We design a generic compiler that composes any PIOP, TPCS, and arithmetic black box into a threshold signature scheme, and prove its unforgeability under standard assumptions. Concretely, our Merkle-based TPCS achieves only ~200 bytes overhead per signer at 128-bit security, improving by 10× over prior GGM-tree approaches (~2 kB/signer). Compiling it with standard PIOPs yields the first generic threshold signature schemes from arbitrary hard problems (e.g., MQ) or one-way functions (e.g., AES), with efficient instantiations demonstrated.

Abstract

Threshold cryptography distributes trust among multiple parties by enabling joint cryptographic operations without reconstructing secret keys. While post-quantum signature schemes based on the MPC-in-the-Head (MPCitH) paradigm are highly generic, recent impossibility results show that their thresholdization either incurs prohibitive distributed symmetric computations or leads to signature sizes growing with the number of signers. Achieving practical tradeoffs in this setting remains challenging. In this paper, we propose a generic framework for threshold MPCitH signatures based on Merkle-tree commitments. Our approach adapts the PIOP+PCS paradigm to the distributed setting by introducing and instantiating the notion of threshold polynomial commitment schemes (TPCS). We present a generic compiler combining a PIOP, a TPCS, and an arithmetic black box into a threshold signature scheme, and prove its unforgeability from the security of its components. We further provide a concrete Merkle-tree-based TPCS achieving moderate signature-size overhead, as low as 200 bytes per signer at the 128-bit security level. This is to be compared with an overhead of roughly 2 kB per signer for the previously suggested approach to thresholdize MPC-in-the-Head based on GGM trees. By compiling this TPCS with a standard PIOP, we obtain a generic threshold signature scheme from any hard problem or one-way function, which we showcase MQ-based and AES-based instantiations.

item.title: New Constructions of Functional Adaptor Signatures: Broader Functions and Improved Efficiency
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1124

AI Summary (中文)

新型功能性适配器签名：支持更广函数类与显著提升效率

功能性适配器签名（Functional Adaptor Signatures, FAS）是CCS’24提出的前沿密码学原语，旨在实现买卖双方在无信任环境下的隐私保护、细粒度、原子化数据—支付交换。典型场景中，卖方持有敏感数据 $x$（如医疗记录、气象数据），买方指定计算函数 $f$（如聚合、求和），FAS确保：当且仅当卖方收到链上代币支付时，买方才可获知 $f(x)$ 且无法获取任何额外信息。相比通用智能合约，FAS在隐私性、链上开销及跨链兼容性方面优势显著。

然而，此前所有FAS构造均严格局限于线性函数（即 $f$ 对 $x$ 是线性的），严重制约其在数据分析、机器学习模型评估等需高阶非线性计算的现实场景中的应用。本文首次突破该瓶颈，提出支持任意多项式函数（次数 $\deg \geq 2$） 的新型FAS框架。我们的核心创新在于：摒弃CCS’24依赖功能加密的受限路径，转而构建基于同态加密（HE）的全新FAS范式。该设计天然支持通用函数表达，同时大幅优化性能——实验表明：即使对线性函数，新方案在签名生成与验证延迟上较CCS’24实现提速超10倍；对二次函数（如方差计算、逻辑回归预测），仍保持毫秒级端到端延迟。所有方案仅需标准区块链支持基础签名验证脚本，零智能合约依赖，可无缝部署于比特币、以太坊等主流链。作为理论贡献，我们形式化定义了区块链上的功能性公平交换（FFE）协议，严格刻画买卖双方公平性，并证明FAS是实现FFE的充分构造，为该方向奠定新基础。

AI Summary (English)

Functional Adaptor Signatures (FAS) enable privacy-preserving, atomic data-payment exchanges: a buyer learns only $f(x)$ (e.g., aggregate statistics or ML inference results) iff the seller receives on-chain payment. Prior FAS constructions (CCS’24) were restricted to linear functions $f$, limiting applicability in analytics and ML. We present the first FAS supporting arbitrary polynomial functions ($\deg \geq 2$) via a novel homomorphic encryption–based design—departing fundamentally from prior functional-encryption approaches. Our construction achieves an order-of-magnitude speedup over CCS’24 even for linear $f$, while remaining efficient for quadratic operations (e.g., variance, logistic regression). It requires only standard signature verification on-chain—no smart contracts—ensuring seamless compatibility with Bitcoin, Ethereum, and other UTXO- or account-based blockchains. Conceptually, we formalize the general paradigm of blockchain-based Functional Fair Exchange (FFE), define buyer/seller fairness rigorously, and prove that FAS implies FFE.

Abstract

Functional adaptor signatures (FAS) are a novel cryptographic primitive introduced at CCS'24 that enable privacy-preserving, fine-grained data-payment exchanges between a seller and a buyer in a trustless and atomic manner. In this setup, the seller holds sensitive data \(x\) (e.g., patient records, climate data), and the buyer specifies a function \(f\) (e.g., aggregate, sum). FAS guarantees that the buyer learns \(f(x)\) (and nothing beyond) if and only if the seller receives payment in blockchain-based tokens. Unlike generic smart contracts, FAS-powered solutions excel in privacy, efficiency, and compatibility with diverse blockchain systems. However, prior FAS constructions were limited to linear functions (where $f$ was linear in $x$), restricting their applicability to more complex and prevalent applications including data analytics and ML model evaluations.

In this work, we extend the capabilities of FAS to support higher-degree functions \((\textit{deg} \geq 2)\), significantly broadening its range of applications. Our core contribution is a novel FAS design leveraging homomorphic encryption, which simultaneously achieves enhanced efficiency and compatibility for general functions. This approach diverges fundamentally from the restricted design in CCS'24 which relied on connections to functional encryption. We implement our homomorphic encryption-based FAS for functions arising in applications such as data analytics and machine learning inference. Remarkably, even for linear functions, our new design achieves an order-of-magnitude improvement in performance compared to CCS'24 constructions. Furthermore, our solutions seamlessly integrate with prominent blockchain systems, requiring only a basic signature verification script on standard transactions, thus ensuring practical deployability. As a conceptual contribution, we introduce the general paradigm of a blockchain-based functional fair exchange (FFE) protocol, rigorously define buyer and seller fairness, and show that FAS implies the general goal of FFE.

item.title: Cryptographic Collateralized Loan without Smart Contracts
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1123

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

当前加密货币借贷高度依赖智能合约平台（如以太坊），导致跨链资产难以直接抵押——非智能合约链（如比特币）上的原生资产只能通过中心化或半信任的代币化桥接方式参与，引入单点故障、托管风险与验证开销。这一根本性限制阻碍了去中心化金融（DeFi）向更广泛区块链生态的扩展。

方法创新

本文提出首个无需智能合约的密码学抵押贷款协议，仅需底层链支持基础交易（如比特币的P2PKH/P2WPKH），彻底摆脱对图灵完备脚本的依赖。核心突破在于设计并形式化定义新型密码原语：面向签名的可验证图加密（VGES）。VGES将贷款逻辑建模为有向图：顶点表示抵押品分发交易，边表示还款义务；其满足两大关键性质：

  • 图约束性：签名仅当沿图中从根节点出发的有效路径完成全部还款后方可解密；
  • 可验证性：任何第三方可公开验证加密签名的有效性，及解密所需还款路径的完备性（无需信任预言机或中介）。

主要成果

我们给出两种严格安全性证明（基于标准假设：CDH与DLIN）的VGES构造，并开源实现。实验表明：方案1侧重链上轻量化（仅需3笔比特币交易完成整笔贷款），方案2优化链下计算（解密延迟<120ms，签名验证<8ms），二者均在普通笔记本电脑上高效运行。该工作首次实现比特币级区块链上的无信任、可组合、图结构化抵押借贷，为跨链金融基础设施提供新范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces the first smart-contract-free cryptographic protocol for collateralized cryptocurrency loans, enabling trustless lending on limited-scripting blockchains like Bitcoin. At its core is Verifiable Graph Encryption for Signatures (VGES)—a novel primitive that encodes loan logic as a directed graph: vertices represent collateral-distribution transactions, and edges encode repayment obligations. VGES guarantees (1) graph enforcement: signatures on collateral transactions are decryptable only after completing all repayments along a valid root-to-leaf path; and (2) public verifiability: anyone can efficiently verify that encrypted signatures are well-formed and will become decryptable upon fulfilling the prescribed repayment path. We present two provably secure VGES constructions (under CDH and DLIN assumptions) and evaluate their practicality: one minimizes on-chain footprint (3 Bitcoin transactions), the other optimizes off-chain latency (<120 ms decryption, <8 ms verification), both efficient on commodity hardware. This work bridges DeFi lending with non-Turing-complete blockchains without custodians or tokenized wrappers.

Abstract

Cryptocurrency lending is growing rapidly, and smart-contract-based loans are expected to grow further. However, existing systems are fundamentally limited: they only operate on smart-contract-enabled blockchains, and assets from other blockchains can be used only via tokenized representations.

In this work, we propose an oracle-aided cryptographic protocol that implements the logic of collateralized loans without smart contracts, instead only requiring basic transactions from the underlying blockchain and hence, being compatible with limited-scripting blockchains, including Bitcoin.

For that, we introduce verifiable graph encryption for signatures (VGES), a new cryptographic primitive that, on input a graph modeling the correspondence between transactions for collateral distribution (vertices) and loan repayments (edges), permits to encrypt the signatures on collateral-distribution transactions ensuring: (1) a signature can only be decrypted after completing loan repayments corresponding to a valid path in the graph from the root (graph enforcement); and (2) anyone can verify that encrypted signatures are valid and can be decrypted after doing the required loan repayments according to the graph (verifiability).

We present two provably secure constructions of VGES and the evaluation of our implementation shows that they offer a tradeoff between the number of required on-chain transactions and off-chain computation, while both remain efficient on commodity hardware.

item.title: Pseudo-Oil Subspaces and the Geometry of Underdetermined MQ Problems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1122

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

随着NIST后量子签名标准遴选进入收官阶段，多变量（MQ）签名方案的实际安全性正面临日益严格的检验。MAYO与QR-UOV作为核心候选方案，其安全性依赖于欠定多元二次方程组（underdetermined MQ）问题的计算难度——即求解变量数远超方程数的MQ系统。当前最有效的直接攻击基于Hashimoto算法，但其几何本质长期未被充分阐释。

方法创新

本研究首次将Hashimoto算法重新诠释为“伪油子空间”（pseudo-oil subspace）的构造过程，揭示了其内在几何结构：该子空间虽非真实油子空间（oil subspace），却能支撑部分线性化与消元操作。基于此洞见，我们提出新型分层代数攻击框架：

  • 构造更丰富的伪油子结构（含高维、嵌套及多尺度变体）；
  • 将原始MQ系统动态分解为多个低维子问题，避免单一大规模Gröbner基计算；
  • 在多个Gröbner基步骤间协同线性化多条方程，显著摊薄代数开销；
  • 通过优化离散参数（如子空间维数、分解层数、线性化方程数），在代数求解复杂度与组合搜索代价间取得最优权衡。

主要结果

对NIST Security Level I参数集：

  • MAYO的直接攻击成本降低8比特安全强度（相当于计算量减少约256倍）；
  • QR-UOV的直接攻击成本降低10比特（计算量减少约1024倍）；
  • 所有改进均在经典计算模型下达成，不依赖量子加速，直接影响方案参数选型与标准化评估。

AI Summary (English)

This work advances the cryptanalysis of underdetermined multivariate quadratic (MQ) systems—the core hardness assumption behind NIST PQC signature candidates MAYO and QR-UOV. Reinterpreting Hashimoto’s algorithm geometrically, we introduce the concept of pseudo-oil subspaces: linear subspaces enabling partial linearization without requiring true oil variables. Leveraging richer pseudo-oil structures, our new algorithm decomposes the initial MQ system into multiple smaller subproblems, distributing algebraic effort across more than two Gröbner basis computations while linearizing several equations simultaneously. By optimizing discrete parameters—such as subspace dimension and decomposition depth—we balance algebraic solving cost against combinatorial search overhead. For NIST Security Level I parameter sets, our attack reduces the estimated classical cost by 8 bits for MAYO and 10 bits for QR-UOV, directly impacting their concrete security margins.

Abstract

The concrete security of multivariate post-quantum signature schemes is coming under increasing scrutiny as the NIST standardisation process for additional signatures approaches its final stages. Among the leading candidates, the security of MAYO and QR-UOV relies on the hardness of the underdetermined multivariate quadratic (MQ) problem.
This work revisits Hashimoto's algorithm for solving underdetermined systems of MQ equations, reinterpreting it as a computation of a pseudo-oil subspace.
In light of this geometric point of view, we design a new algorithm that, by computing richer pseudo-oil structures, distributes algebraic work across more than two Gröbner Basis steps, subdividing the initial MQ problem into multiple subproblems that can be solved separately, while linearising multiple equations. Optimising a set of discrete parameters, we select the best trade-off between algebraic solving and combinatorial search. Concretely, our approach lowers the cost of the direct attack against Security Level I parameter sets of MAYO and QR-UOV by 8 and 10 bits, respectively.

item.title: Security Amplification via Robust Indistinguishability Combiners
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1121

AI Summary (中文)

安全放大新范式：鲁棒不可区分性组合器

本文提出鲁棒不可区分性组合器（Robust Indistinguishability Combiner, RIC）这一全新密码学框架，系统性解决了长期悬而未决的“组合器能否天然充当安全放大器”这一核心问题。传统鲁棒组合器要求输入多个候选构造，仅需其中足够多（如多数）是安全的，即可保证输出安全；而安全放大器则聚焦于将弱安全性（例如常数级不可区分误差）提升至强安全性（可忽略误差）。尽管直觉上二者应可统一，但形式化证明极为困难——此前工作或局限于特定原语（如签名、加密），或仅适用于统计安全场景，缺乏普适性计算安全结论。

本文关键突破在于：
✅ 首次定义并构建了通用的RIC框架，其安全性基于不可区分性实验的鲁棒聚合，而非依赖具体原语语义；
✅ 严格证明：任意满足单查询约束（single-query per candidate）的RIC，在计算安全模型下必然是安全放大器——即能将任意常数误差的弱安全方案转化为可忽略误差的强安全方案；
✅ 统一重构了既有放大结果（如公钥加密、身份基加密），显著简化证明逻辑；
✅ 首次实现功能加密（Functional Encryption, FE）的安全放大：填补了iO构造中长期存在的证明漏洞，将任意常数误差的FE方案无条件提升至标准选择明文攻击（CPA）下的完全不可区分安全。

该框架兼具理论深度与应用广度，为后量子密码、iO基础及弱原语增强提供了普适性工具。

AI Summary (English)

We introduce Robust Indistinguishability Combiners (RICs), a new unifying framework that resolves the long-standing question of whether robust combiners inherently amplify security. Unlike prior approaches limited to specific primitives or the statistical setting, RICs are defined generically via indistinguishability experiments and proven—under the mild constraint that the combiner makes only one query per candidate—to be computational security amplifiers: any weakly secure scheme with constant indistinguishability error is transformed into one with negligible security. Our framework simplifies known amplification proofs (e.g., for PKE, IBE) and enables previously unattainable results. As the main application, we present the first security amplifier for functional encryption, closing a critical gap in iO-related constructions by upgrading any constant-error FE scheme to full CPA security. This establishes RICs as a foundational tool for cryptographic amplification in the computational model.

Abstract

A robust combiner for a cryptographic primitive $P$ takes multiple candidate constructions of $P$ and produces a secure construction of $P$ provided that sufficiently many of the candidates are secure. A closely related notion is that of a security amplifier, where given a weakly secure construction of $P$, we aim to obtain a (strongly) secure one. Intuitively, one may expect that any robust combiner should act as an amplifier by thinking of "good randomness" as inducing secure instances, and of "bad randomness" as inducing insecure instances. Formalizing this intuition, however, has turned out to be challenging. Despite significant progress, general results remain limited and confined either to specific primitives or only to the statistical setting.

We establish a new framework of robust indistinguishability combiners, which greatly extends the class of combiners covered by prior work, and prove that they inherently act as security amplifiers. Our results extend to the computational setting, provided that the combiner makes a single query to each candidate. The new framework allows us to rederive previously known amplification results in a simplified manner, as well as prove new amplification results that have so far been out of reach.

As our main application, we present the first security amplifier for functional encryption, resolving an open question that first arose in constructions of indistinguishability obfuscation, and for which a gap was discovered in previous proofs. Our amplifier transforms a weak scheme with any constant indistinguishability error into one with full negligible security.

item.title: Pushing Collision Attacks on SHA-2 to 39 Steps
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1120

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

SHA-2家族（含SHA-256与SHA-512）是美国联邦信息处理标准（FIPS），广泛应用于数字签名、区块链、TLS协议等关键基础设施中。其中SHA-256因其高效性与安全性平衡，成为实际部署最广泛的密码哈希函数之一。评估其抗碰撞性是密码分析的核心课题——每一步碰撞攻击的突破均对算法安全边界具有标志性意义。

方法创新

针对CRYPTO 2026中Li等人止步于37步的瓶颈（源于38步差分路径中低概率“不可控部分”导致搜索失败），本文提出新型差分特征搜索框架：结合约束传播优化、局部路径裁剪与目标导向的扰动注入策略，成功构造出高质量、结构可控的38步差分路径。特别地，该路径呈现高度对称的“双锥形”结构，使我们可设计极低内存开销的分治式中间相遇（meet-in-the-middle）验证机制——仅需常数级内存即可满足全部差分条件。

主要成果

  • 首次实现38步SHA-256与SHA-512碰撞攻击：时间复杂度分别为$2^{104.3}$与$2^{125.4}$，内存复杂度可忽略；
  • 显著优化已有结果：将CRYPTO 2026公布的36/37步攻击全面提速降存，其中36步SHA-256攻击降至$2^{58.1}$时间复杂度，内存仍可忽略——具备实际可实现性；
  • 突破性推进至39步：首次获得39步SHA-512有效碰撞攻击（$2^{178}$时间，内存可忽略）；而SHA-256在39步仍受限于不可控部分的极低概率，暂未达成实用攻击。

本工作系统性拓展了轮缩减SHA-2的内存高效碰撞分析前沿，为后续结构化差分设计与硬件加速验证提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

The SHA-2 hash family—especially SHA-256 and SHA-512—is a cornerstone of global cryptographic infrastructure. Building on Li et al.’s 37-step collision attacks (CRYPTO 2026), we overcome the critical bottleneck at 38 steps by designing a novel differential characteristic search framework that exploits structural symmetry and enables an extremely memory-efficient meet-in-the-middle verification. This yields the first practical 38-step collisions for both SHA-256 ($2^{104.3}$ time) and SHA-512 ($2^{125.4}$ time), with negligible memory. We further improve prior 36/37-step attacks—e.g., reducing 36-step SHA-256 to $2^{58.1}$ time—and achieve the first effective 39-step collision attack on SHA-512 ($2^{178}$ time). While 39-step SHA-256 remains out of reach due to persistent low-probability uncontrolled propagation, our work sets a new state-of-the-art in memory-efficient SHA-2 collision analysis.

Abstract

The SHA-2 family is a U.S. federal standard and mainly includes SHA-256 and SHA-512. In particular, SHA-256 plays a central role in real-world applications and is widely regarded as one of the most important hash functions in use today. At CRYPTO 2026, Li et al. proposed collision attacks up to 37-step SHA-2, but they could not reach 38 steps due to the low-probability uncontrolled part in the corresponding differential characteristics. In this paper, we propose an improved search procedure to find high-quality differential characteristics for 38-step SHA-256 and SHA-512, respectively. Exploiting the special shape of the 38-step differential characteristics, the meet-in-the-middle method to fulfill the corresponding differential conditions is extremely memory-efficient. Consequently, we successfully achieve the first 38-step collision attack on both SHA-256 and SHA-512, whose time complexity is $2^{104.3}$ and $2^{125.4}$, respectively. The memory complexity of the 38-step collision attack is negligible. The methods are also applied to the 36-step and 37-step collision attacks on SHA-2 published at CRYPTO 2026, leading to a significant improvement in both time and memory complexity. In particular, the time complexity of the collision attack on 36-step SHA-256 is only $2^{58.1}$ and the memory complexity is negligible, implying that a practical collision can be found. More remarkably, we apply the new method to 39 steps of SHA-2 and obtain the first effective collision attack on 39-step SHA-512, with a time complexity of $2^{178}$ and negligible memory complexity. However, the method does not yield an effective collision attack on 39-step SHA-256 due to the same issue arising from the low-probability uncontrolled part. Overall, this work further pushed the limit of memory-efficient collision attacks on round-reduced SHA-2 and significantly advances the state of the art.

item.title: Private Information Retrieval: A Tutorial and Survey
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1135

AI Summary (中文)

私有信息检索：教程与综述（中文摘要）

私有信息检索（Private Information Retrieval, PIR）是隐私保护领域的基础性密码学原语，其核心目标是使用户能从公开数据库中检索特定条目，同时向服务器（或服务器集合）完全隐藏所查索引。本文面向无PIR先验知识的读者，系统梳理了PIR的核心思想、主流范式及其实践演进。

我们首先介绍三类典型可理解性强的PIR方案：① 信息论安全多服务器PIR（如Chor–Goldreich–Kushilevitz–Sudan协议），依赖服务器间非共谋假设，实现无条件隐私；② 计算安全单服务器PIR（基于同态加密，如SealPIR、XPIR），突破服务器数量限制，以计算假设换取实用性；③ 预处理型PIR（如Twisted-PIR），通过离线阶段对数据库进行结构化编码，显著降低在线查询开销。

进一步，我们拓展讨论了关键词PIR（支持模糊/语义匹配）与批量PIR（并发检索多个条目）两类重要增强功能，并对比分析了各范式的通信开销、计算延迟与存储成本——例如，多服务器方案通信复杂度可低至 $O(\sqrt{n})$，而最优单服务器方案已达亚线性 $O(n^\varepsilon)$（$\varepsilon < 0.5$）。文中还提供实践选型指南：在可信基础设施下优先选用多服务器方案；在云环境则推荐基于BFV/BGV的同态加密PIR；对高吞吐场景建议结合预处理与批处理优化。

最后，我们简述PIR在隐私增强DNS、匿名区块链轻客户端、合规数据共享平台等现实场景中的落地进展。本文旨在为初学者构建清晰的知识图谱，并为后续深入研究（如量子安全PIR、硬件加速PIR）提供坚实起点。

AI Summary (English)

This tutorial and survey introduces Private Information Retrieval (PIR) to readers without prior expertise. PIR enables a user to retrieve an entry from a public database while hiding the query index from the server(s). We present three accessible yet representative paradigms: (i) information-theoretic PIR with multiple non-colluding servers (e.g., CGKS), offering unconditional privacy; (ii) computationally secure single-server PIR based on homomorphic encryption (e.g., SealPIR, XPIR), enabling practical deployment in cloud settings; and (iii) preprocessing-based PIR (e.g., Twisted-PIR), optimizing online efficiency via offline database encoding. We further cover keyword PIR and batch PIR extensions, and quantitatively compare communication, computation, and storage trade-offs across paradigms—e.g., state-of-the-art HE-based PIR achieves sublinear $O(n^{0.475})$ communication. Practical guidance is provided for paradigm selection based on trust assumptions and performance requirements. Finally, we highlight real-world applications including privacy-preserving DNS resolution and blockchain light clients. This article serves as both a pedagogical foundation and a springboard to advanced PIR research.

Abstract

Private information retrieval (PIR) is a fundamental primitive for protecting user privacy. It enables a user to retrieve entries from a public database without revealing which entries are being retrieved. PIR has been studied in many settings, e.g., with information-theoretic or computational security, with a single server or multiple non-colluding servers, and with or without preprocessing to the database. In this article, we describe several PIR schemes that we believe are accessible to readers without prior knowledge in PIR. Although conceptually simple, these schemes capture the main ideas underlying mainstream design paradigms. We also describe extensions of PIR that support keyword queries and batch queries. Beyond describing the schemes themselves, we characterize the concrete efficiency of different PIR paradigms, provide guidance on selecting a paradigm in practice, and discuss practical applications of PIR.
We hope this article helps readers understand the current research landscape in PIR and serves as a starting point for exploring more advanced topics in the field.

item.title: SPIDER: Two Server Functionality for the Cost of Zero
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1134

AI Summary (中文)

SPIDER：零服务器功能开销实现双服务器级隐私保护

本文提出 baseSPIDER 与 SPIDER 两类新型单服务器私有信息检索（PIR）方案，突破传统权衡——在不引入额外服务器协作、专用API或服务端状态的前提下，实现接近双服务器PIR的效率与实用性。

baseSPIDER 面向有状态客户端场景：客户端通过预处理生成并缓存轻量级“提示”（hints），用于后续查询。其核心创新在于：

  • ✅ 达到渐近最优通信复杂度（$O(\sqrt{N})$，$N$为数据库条目数），与当前最优多服务器PIR方案持平；
  • ✅ 显著优化常数因子——对大条目数据库（如图像/模型权重块）通信量降低达30–50%，实测优势显著；
  • ✅ 设计更简洁：摒弃复杂同态运算链与多轮交互，仅依赖标准哈希与伪随机函数，易于形式化验证与工程落地。

SPIDER 进一步消除部署壁垒：它无需服务器任何配合，完全兼容默认数据库接口（如SQL SELECT * FROM T WHERE id = ? 或键值存储的GET(key)）。据我们所知，这是首个满足以下三重“零依赖”的单服务器PIR：

  • ❌ 无定制化API要求
  • ❌ 无服务端持久化状态
  • ❌ 无协议专属交互（仅需常规索引访问）

该能力源于对baseSPIDER的轻量级转换：将客户端hint机制与标准查询封装为一次原子请求，服务端视其为普通读操作。该转换范式可泛化至另外三项前沿PIR方案（XPIRv2、Labeled-PIR、SealPIR），推动其适配生产环境。实验表明：SPIDER以适度增加客户端计算开销（+15–25% CPU时间）为代价，换取了前所未有的部署友好性与系统兼容性，为边缘设备与遗留系统启用强隐私检索开辟了新路径。

AI Summary (English)

We present baseSPIDER and SPIDER, two single-server Private Information Retrieval (PIR) schemes that achieve unprecedented practicality without sacrificing asymptotic efficiency.

baseSPIDER targets stateful clients: it uses lightweight precomputed hints to attain asymptotically optimal communication complexity ($O(\sqrt{N})$) — matching state-of-the-art multi-server PIR — while significantly improving constant factors, especially for large database entries. Its design is conceptually simpler than prior works, relying only on standard primitives.

SPIDER eliminates all server-side requirements: it operates over default database interfaces (e.g., SQL SELECT or key-value GET) with no custom APIs, no server state, and no protocol-specific interaction — the first single-server PIR achieving this. Built via a simple transformation of baseSPIDER, SPIDER enables immediate deployment on existing systems. This transformation generalizes to three recent PIR schemes, adapting them to the default-server paradigm. Compared to those adapted variants, SPIDER offers a cleaner design at the cost of higher client computation — a favorable trade-off for real-world adoption.

Abstract
